1Ôćĺ"""
     2ÔćĺBrowser action recorder - connects to existing Chrome via CDP
     3Ôćĺand records user interactions as replayable Playwright code.
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺUsage:
     6Ôćĺ    python3 scripts/recorder.py [--cdp http://localhost:9222] [-o recorded_actions.py]
     7Ôćĺ
     8ÔćĺPress Ctrl+C to stop recording.
     9Ôćĺ"""
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺimport asyncio
    12Ôćĺimport json
    13Ôćĺimport sys
    14Ôćĺimport signal
    15Ôćĺimport time
    16Ôćĺfrom datetime import datetime
    17Ôćĺfrom pathlib import Path
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ# Add project root to path
    20Ôćĺsys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺfrom patchright.async_api import async_playwright
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ# JS snippet injected into the page to capture user interactions
    26ÔćĺRECORDER_JS = """
    27Ôćĺ(() => {
    28Ôćĺ    if (window.__recorder_installed__) return;
    29Ôćĺ    window.__recorder_installed__ = true;
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ    const events = [];
    32Ôćĺ    const startTime = Date.now();
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ    function getSelector(el) {
    35Ôćĺ        // Priority 1: data-testid or id
    36Ôćĺ        if (el.id) return '#' + CSS.escape(el.id);
    37Ôćĺ        if (el.dataset && el.dataset.testid) return `[data-testid="${el.dataset.testid}"]`;
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ        // Priority 2: unique text content for buttons/links
    40Ôćĺ        const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase();
    41Ôćĺ        if (['button', 'a', 'span', 'div', 'p', 'h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'h4', 'li'].includes(tag)) {
    42Ôćĺ            const text = el.innerText?.trim();
    43Ôćĺ            if (text && text.length < 50 && text.length > 0) {
    44Ôćĺ                return `text=${JSON.stringify(text)}`;
    45Ôćĺ            }
    46Ôćĺ        }
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ        // Priority 3: input by placeholder or name
    49Ôćĺ        if (tag === 'input' || tag === 'textarea') {
    50Ôćĺ            if (el.placeholder) return `${tag}[placeholder="${el.placeholder}"]`;
    51Ôćĺ            if (el.name) return `${tag}[name="${el.name}"]`;
    52Ôćĺ            if (el.type) return `${tag}[type="${el.type}"]`;
    53Ôćĺ        }
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ        // Priority 4: CSS path
    56Ôćĺ        const path = [];
    57Ôćĺ        let current = el;
    58Ôćĺ        while (current && current !== document.body) {
    59Ôćĺ            let segment = current.tagName.toLowerCase();
    60Ôćĺ            if (current.className && typeof current.className === 'string') {
    61Ôćĺ                const classes = current.className.trim().split(/\\s+/).filter(c => c.length > 0 && c.length < 30);
    62Ôćĺ                if (classes.length > 0) {
    63Ôćĺ                    segment += '.' + classes.slice(0, 2).map(c => CSS.escape(c)).join('.');
    64Ôćĺ                }
    65Ôćĺ            }
    66Ôćĺ            path.unshift(segment);
    67Ôćĺ            current = current.parentElement;
    68Ôćĺ            if (path.length >= 4) break;
    69Ôćĺ        }
    70Ôćĺ        return path.join(' > ');
    71Ôćĺ    }
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ    function emit(type, detail) {
    74Ôćĺ        const data = {
    75Ôćĺ            type: type,
    76Ôćĺ            timestamp: Date.now() - startTime,
    77Ôćĺ            url: location.href,
    78Ôćĺ            ...detail
    79Ôćĺ        };
    80Ôćĺ        console.log('__RECORDER__:' + JSON.stringify(data));
    81Ôćĺ    }
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ    // Click events
    84Ôćĺ    document.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
    85Ôćĺ        const el = e.target;
    86Ôćĺ        const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
    87Ôćĺ        emit('click', {
    88Ôćĺ            selector: getSelector(el),
    89Ôćĺ            tag: el.tagName.toLowerCase(),
    90Ôćĺ            text: (el.innerText || '').trim().substring(0, 80),
    91Ôćĺ            x: Math.round(e.clientX),
    92Ôćĺ            y: Math.round(e.clientY),
    93Ôćĺ            elementX: Math.round(rect.x),
    94Ôćĺ            elementY: Math.round(rect.y),
    95Ôćĺ            elementW: Math.round(rect.width),
    96Ôćĺ            elementH: Math.round(rect.height)
    97Ôćĺ        });
    98Ôćĺ    }, true);
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ    // Input events (debounced)
   101Ôćĺ    let inputTimers = new Map();
   102Ôćĺ    document.addEventListener('input', (e) => {
   103Ôćĺ        const el = e.target;
   104Ôćĺ        const key = getSelector(el);
   105Ôćĺ        if (inputTimers.has(key)) clearTimeout(inputTimers.get(key));
   106Ôćĺ        inputTimers.set(key, setTimeout(() => {
   107Ôćĺ            emit('input', {
   108Ôćĺ                selector: key,
   109Ôćĺ                tag: el.tagName.toLowerCase(),
   110Ôćĺ                value: el.value,
   111Ôćĺ                placeholder: el.placeholder || ''
   112Ôćĺ            });
   113Ôćĺ            inputTimers.delete(key);
   114Ôćĺ        }, 500));
   115Ôćĺ    }, true);
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ    // Keyboard: Enter and Escape
   118Ôćĺ    document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
   119Ôćĺ        if (['Enter', 'Escape', 'Tab'].includes(e.key)) {
   120Ôćĺ            emit('keydown', {
   121Ôćĺ                key: e.key,
   122Ôćĺ                selector: getSelector(e.target)
   123Ôćĺ            });
   124Ôćĺ        }
   125Ôćĺ    }, true);
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ    // Scroll events (debounced)
   128Ôćĺ    let scrollTimer = null;
   129Ôćĺ    window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
   130Ôćĺ        if (scrollTimer) clearTimeout(scrollTimer);
   131Ôćĺ        scrollTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   132Ôćĺ            emit('scroll', {
   133Ôćĺ                scrollX: Math.round(window.scrollX),
   134Ôćĺ                scrollY: Math.round(window.scrollY)
   135Ôćĺ            });
   136Ôćĺ        }, 300);
   137Ôćĺ    }, true);
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ    // Detect navigation via History API
   140Ôćĺ    const origPush = history.pushState;
   141Ôćĺ    const origReplace = history.replaceState;
   142Ôćĺ    history.pushState = function() {
   143Ôćĺ        origPush.apply(this, arguments);
   144Ôćĺ        emit('navigation', { url: location.href, method: 'pushState' });
   145Ôćĺ    };
   146Ôćĺ    history.replaceState = function() {
   147Ôćĺ        origReplace.apply(this, arguments);
   148Ôćĺ        emit('navigation', { url: location.href, method: 'replaceState' });
   149Ôćĺ    };
   150Ôćĺ    window.addEventListener('popstate', () => {
   151Ôćĺ        emit('navigation', { url: location.href, method: 'popstate' });
   152Ôćĺ    });
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ    console.log('__RECORDER__:' + JSON.stringify({type: 'ready', timestamp: 0, url: location.href}));
   155Ôćĺ})();
   156Ôćĺ"""
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺclass ActionRecorder:
   160Ôćĺ    def __init__(self, cdp_url="http://localhost:9222", output_file=None):
   161Ôćĺ        self.cdp_url = cdp_url
   162Ôćĺ        self.output_file = output_file or f"recorded_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}.py"
   163Ôćĺ        self.events = []
   164Ôćĺ        self.running = True
   165Ôćĺ        self.page = None
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ    def _format_event(self, event):
   168Ôćĺ        """Format a single event for display."""
   169Ôćĺ        t = event.get("type", "?")
   170Ôćĺ        ts = event.get("timestamp", 0)
   171Ôćĺ        sec = ts / 1000
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ        if t == "click":
   174Ôćĺ            text = event.get("text", "")[:40]
   175Ôćĺ            sel = event.get("selector", "")
   176Ôćĺ            return f"[{sec:6.1f}s] CLICK  {sel}  {f'({text})' if text else ''}"
   177Ôćĺ        elif t == "input":
   178Ôćĺ            sel = event.get("selector", "")
   179Ôćĺ            val = event.get("value", "")[:40]
   180Ôćĺ            return f"[{sec:6.1f}s] INPUT  {sel}  = \"{val}\""
   181Ôćĺ        elif t == "keydown":
   182Ôćĺ            return f"[{sec:6.1f}s] KEY    {event.get('key', '')}"
   183Ôćĺ        elif t == "scroll":
   184Ôćĺ            return f"[{sec:6.1f}s] SCROLL y={event.get('scrollY', 0)}"
   185Ôćĺ        elif t == "navigation":
   186Ôćĺ            return f"[{sec:6.1f}s] NAV    {event.get('url', '')}"
   187Ôćĺ        elif t == "page_navigation":
   188Ôćĺ            return f"[{sec:6.1f}s] PAGE   {event.get('url', '')}"
   189Ôćĺ        elif t == "ready":
   190Ôćĺ            return f"[{sec:6.1f}s] READY  Recording on {event.get('url', '')}"
   191Ôćĺ        return f"[{sec:6.1f}s] {t}"
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ    def _generate_code(self):
   194Ôćĺ        """Generate replayable Python code from recorded events."""
   195Ôćĺ        lines = [
   196Ôćĺ            '"""Auto-generated by recorder.py"""',
   197Ôćĺ            "",
   198Ôćĺ            "import asyncio",
   199Ôćĺ            "from patchright.async_api import async_playwright",
   200Ôćĺ            "",
   201Ôćĺ            "",
   202Ôćĺ            "async def replay(cdp_url='http://localhost:9222'):",
   203Ôćĺ            "    p = await async_playwright().start()",
   204Ôćĺ            "    browser = await p.chromium.connect_over_cdp(cdp_url)",
   205Ôćĺ            "    context = browser.contexts[0]",
   206Ôćĺ            "    page = context.pages[0] if context.pages else await context.new_page()",
   207Ôćĺ            "",
   208Ôćĺ        ]
   209Ôćĺ
   210Ôćĺ        prev_ts = 0
   211Ôćĺ        for ev in self.events:
   212Ôćĺ            if ev["type"] in ("ready",):
   213Ôćĺ                continue
   214Ôćĺ
   215Ôćĺ            ts = ev.get("timestamp", 0)
   216Ôćĺ            delay = ts - prev_ts
   217Ôćĺ            if delay > 500:
   218Ôćĺ                lines.append(f"    await asyncio.sleep({delay / 1000:.1f})")
   219Ôćĺ            prev_ts = ts
   220Ôćĺ
   221Ôćĺ            t = ev["type"]
   222Ôćĺ            if t == "navigation" or t == "page_navigation":
   223Ôćĺ                url = ev.get("url", "")
   224Ôćĺ                lines.append(f"    await page.goto({url!r}, wait_until='domcontentloaded')")
   225Ôćĺ            elif t == "click":
   226Ôćĺ                sel = ev.get("selector", "")
   227Ôćĺ                lines.append(f"    await page.locator({sel!r}).click()")
   228Ôćĺ            elif t == "input":
   229Ôćĺ                sel = ev.get("selector", "")
   230Ôćĺ                val = ev.get("value", "")
   231Ôćĺ                lines.append(f"    await page.locator({sel!r}).fill({val!r})")
   232Ôćĺ            elif t == "keydown":
   233Ôćĺ                key = ev.get("key", "")
   234Ôćĺ                lines.append(f"    await page.keyboard.press({key!r})")
   235Ôćĺ            elif t == "scroll":
   236Ôćĺ                sy = ev.get("scrollY", 0)
   237Ôćĺ                lines.append(f"    await page.evaluate('window.scrollTo(0, {sy})')")
   238Ôćĺ
   239Ôćĺ        lines.extend([
   240Ôćĺ            "",
   241Ôćĺ            "",
   242Ôćĺ            "if __name__ == '__main__':",
   243Ôćĺ            "    asyncio.run(replay())",
   244Ôćĺ            "",
   245Ôćĺ        ])
   246Ôćĺ        return "\n".join(lines)
   247Ôćĺ
   248Ôćĺ    async def _on_console(self, msg):
   249Ôćĺ        """Handle console messages from the injected recorder."""
   250Ôćĺ        text = msg.text
   251Ôćĺ        if not text.startswith("__RECORDER__:"):
   252Ôćĺ            return
   253Ôćĺ        try:
   254Ôćĺ            data = json.loads(text[len("__RECORDER__:"):])
   255Ôćĺ            self.events.append(data)
   256Ôćĺ            print(self._format_event(data))
   257Ôćĺ        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   258Ôćĺ            pass
   259Ôćĺ
   260Ôćĺ    async def _inject_recorder(self, page):
   261Ôćĺ        """Inject the recorder script into a page."""
   262Ôćĺ        try:
   263Ôćĺ            await page.evaluate(RECORDER_JS)
   264Ôćĺ        except Exception as e:
   265Ôćĺ            print(f"  Warning: could not inject recorder into page: {e}")
   266Ôćĺ
   267Ôćĺ    async def run(self):
   268Ôćĺ        print(f"Connecting to Chrome at {self.cdp_url} ...")
   269Ôćĺ        async with async_playwright() as p:
   270Ôćĺ            browser = await p.chromium.connect_over_cdp(self.cdp_url)
   271Ôćĺ            context = browser.contexts[0]
   272Ôćĺ
   273Ôćĺ            # Inject into all existing pages
   274Ôćĺ            for page in context.pages:
   275Ôćĺ                page.on("console", self._on_console)
   276Ôćĺ                page.on("framenavigated", lambda frame, pg=page: asyncio.ensure_future(self._on_frame_navigated(pg, frame)))
   277Ôćĺ                await self._inject_recorder(page)
   278Ôćĺ                print(f"  Attached to: {page.url}")
   279Ôćĺ
   280Ôćĺ            # Handle new pages (tabs)
   281Ôćĺ            def on_new_page(page):
   282Ôćĺ                page.on("console", self._on_console)
   283Ôćĺ                page.on("framenavigated", lambda frame, pg=page: asyncio.ensure_future(self._on_frame_navigated(pg, frame)))
   284Ôćĺ                asyncio.ensure_future(self._inject_recorder(page))
   285Ôćĺ                print(f"  New tab: {page.url}")
   286Ôćĺ
   287Ôćĺ            context.on("page", on_new_page)
   288Ôćĺ
   289Ôćĺ            print()
   290Ôćĺ            print("=" * 60)
   291Ôćĺ            print("  RECORDING ÔÇö operate in Chrome normally")
   292Ôćĺ            print("  Press Ctrl+C to stop and save")
   293Ôćĺ            print("=" * 60)
   294Ôćĺ            print()
   295Ôćĺ
   296Ôćĺ            # Keep running until Ctrl+C
   297Ôćĺ            try:
   298Ôćĺ                while self.running:
   299Ôćĺ                    await asyncio.sleep(0.5)
   300Ôćĺ            except asyncio.CancelledError:
   301Ôćĺ                pass
   302Ôćĺ
   303Ôćĺ            # Save
   304Ôćĺ            self._save()
   305Ôćĺ            await browser.close()
   306Ôćĺ
   307Ôćĺ    async def _on_frame_navigated(self, page, frame):
   308Ôćĺ        """Re-inject recorder after navigation."""
   309Ôćĺ        if frame == page.main_frame:
   310Ôćĺ            self.events.append({
   311Ôćĺ                "type": "page_navigation",
   312Ôćĺ                "timestamp": int(time.time() * 1000) - (self.events[0]["timestamp"] if self.events else 0),
   313Ôćĺ                "url": page.url
   314Ôćĺ            })
   315Ôćĺ            print(self._format_event(self.events[-1]))
   316Ôćĺ            await asyncio.sleep(1)  # Wait for page load
   317Ôćĺ            await self._inject_recorder(page)
   318Ôćĺ
   319Ôćĺ    def _save(self):
   320Ôćĺ        if not self.events:
   321Ôćĺ            print("\nNo events recorded.")
   322Ôćĺ            return
   323Ôćĺ
   324Ôćĺ        # Save raw events as JSON
   325Ôćĺ        json_file = self.output_file.replace(".py", ".json")
   326Ôćĺ        with open(json_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   327Ôćĺ            json.dump(self.events, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
   328Ôćĺ        print(f"\nRaw events saved to: {json_file}")
   329Ôćĺ
   330Ôćĺ        # Save as Python code
   331Ôćĺ        code = self._generate_code()
   332Ôćĺ        with open(self.output_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   333Ôćĺ            f.write(code)
   334Ôćĺ        print(f"Replay script saved to: {self.output_file}")
   335Ôćĺ        print(f"Total events: {len(self.events)}")
   336Ôćĺ
   337Ôćĺ
   338Ôćĺdef main():
   339Ôćĺ    import argparse
   340Ôćĺ    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Record browser actions via CDP")
   341Ôćĺ    parser.add_argument("--cdp", default="http://localhost:9222", help="CDP endpoint")
   342Ôćĺ    parser.add_argument("-o", "--output", default=None, help="Output file (default: recorded_<timestamp>.py)")
   343Ôćĺ    args = parser.parse_args()
   344Ôćĺ
   345Ôćĺ    recorder = ActionRecorder(cdp_url=args.cdp, output_file=args.output)
   346Ôćĺ
   347Ôćĺ    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
   348Ôćĺ
   349Ôćĺ    def handle_sigint(*_):
   350Ôćĺ        recorder.running = False
   351Ôćĺ
   352Ôćĺ    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, handle_sigint)
   353Ôćĺ
   354Ôćĺ    try:
   355Ôćĺ        loop.run_until_complete(recorder.run())
   356Ôćĺ    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   357Ôćĺ        recorder._save()
   358Ôćĺ
   359Ôćĺ
   360Ôćĺif __name__ == "__main__":
   361Ôćĺ    main()
   362Ôćĺ

I have now thoroughly read every file in the codebase. Let me compile my findings. --- ## Engineering Defects Report After a thorough scan of every file in the codebase, here are the NEW defects found, organized by category. --- ### 1. BUGS AND LOGIC ERRORS **[BUG-1] Race condition: `_last_action_time` ignores naive/aware datetime mixing in rate limiter comparison** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/rate_limiter.py`, line 170-172 - **Issue:** `_get_last_action_time` queries `func.max(ActionLog.timestamp)` from SQLite. SQLite stores timestamps as strings and SQLAlchemy returns naive datetimes from SQLite by default. The result `last_action` is compared against `now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)` (timezone-aware). This `(now - last_action).total_seconds()` will raise `TypeError: can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes` on Python 3. - **Relevant code:** ```python # line 123 now = datetime.now(timezone.utc) # aware # line 169 last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type=action_type) # naive from SQLite # line 171 elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds() # TypeError! ``` - **Note:** The same issue exists at lines 127-128 and 148-149 where `_count_actions` is called with an aware `since` datetime but the database `ActionLog.timestamp` column stores naive values from `_utcnow()` - the default function returns aware, but existing data or data from direct `ActionLog(timestamp=...)` may vary. However, the `_get_last_action_time` path is the most dangerous because it returns a raw SQLAlchemy scalar and directly subtracts. **[BUG-2] `send_message` non-interactive mode never passes `interactive=False` to chat flow** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/message.py`, lines 95-101 - **Issue:** When `send_message()` is called from the web route via `messages.py:send_msg`, it does not pass `interactive=False`. The function defaults `interactive=True` (line 81). When `interactive=True` and `sys.stdin.isatty()` returns `False` (common in FastAPI/uvicorn), `confirm` becomes `False` (line 154), and the message is cancelled. This means web single-message sends via `/messages/send` always get cancelled. - **Relevant code:** ```python # src/web/routes/messages.py line 95 result = await send_message( boss_id=boss_id, template_name=template_name, variables=variables, preview_only=False, browser=browser, # missing: interactive=False ) ``` **[BUG-3] Checkbox form values not parsed correctly for `skip_messaged` and `dry_run`** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/pipeline.py`, lines 66-67 - **Issue:** HTML checkboxes submit `"true"` as a string when checked, and are absent from the form data when unchecked. `Form(True)` and `Form(False)` defaults do not behave as expected: - For `skip_messaged: bool = Form(True)`: When unchecked, the browser sends nothing for this field, so the default `True` is used. Correct behavior. - When checked, the browser sends `"true"` (string). FastAPI Form coerces this to `True`. Correct. - For `dry_run: bool = Form(False)`: When checked, sends `"true"`, coerces to `True`. Correct. - BUT: `include_messages` in export uses the same pattern and the issue is that `Form(True)` for `include_messages` actually has the same unchecked-means-absent problem. When unchecked, the value defaults to `True` per `Form(True)`, making it impossible to export without messages. - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/export.py`, line 24 ```python include_messages: bool = Form(True), # unchecked checkbox = absent = True (impossible to disable) ``` **[BUG-4] `_scroll_search_results` JavaScript has a syntax error in multi-line template string** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/search.py`, lines 452-453 - **Issue:** The JavaScript string passed to `page.evaluate()` uses a Python triple-quoted string, but line 452-453 has a line break in the middle of a JS string literal inside `querySelectorAll()`: ```python const containers = document.querySelectorAll( '[class*="recommend-list"], [class*="geek-list"], [class*="search-result"], [class*="candidate-list"], [class*="card-list"], [class*="scroll-container"]' ); ``` This is a multi-line JavaScript string literal. In JavaScript, a string literal enclosed in single quotes cannot span lines. When `page.evaluate()` sends this to Chrome, it will produce a SyntaxError. The fix would be to concatenate the strings or use template literals. **[BUG-5] Export datetime formatting silently drops NaT values** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/export.py`, line 220 - **Issue:** `.dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M').replace('NaT', '')` - `strftime` applied to a Series produces NaT for missing values, but `.replace('NaT', '')` uses pandas string `replace` which only replaces the literal substring `'NaT'` within each cell string, not cells that ARE the string 'NaT'. The correct approach would be `.dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M').fillna('')` or `.where(df[col].notna(), '')`. **[BUG-6] `boss_flow_search.py` has absurd sleep duration** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/boss_flow_search.py`, line 15 - **Issue:** `await asyncio.sleep(1770796716.7)` -- this is ~56 years. The recorder captured an actual timestamp delta here instead of a reasonable delay. If anyone runs this script, it hangs forever. --- ### 2. SECURITY ISSUES **[SEC-1] SQL injection via ILIKE with unsanitized user input** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/candidates.py`, lines 28-33 - **Issue:** The search parameter `q` is interpolated into a LIKE pattern with `f"%{q}%"`. If the user provides `%` or `_` characters, they become LIKE wildcards and alter the query. This is LIKE injection. While it is not full SQL injection (parameterized queries are used), it allows the user to craft patterns that match unintended rows or cause performance degradation. ```python like = f"%{q}%" query = query.filter( Candidate.name.ilike(like) | Candidate.title.ilike(like) | Candidate.company.ilike(like) ) ``` **[SEC-2] Settings endpoint allows arbitrary JSON writes with no validation or authentication** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/settings.py`, lines 28-49 - **Issue:** The `POST /settings` endpoint accepts any JSON and writes it directly to `settings.json` without any validation. An attacker (or accidental user action) could: - Delete all settings (empty JSON) - Add malicious entries - Overwrite rate limits to zero (disabling all protections) - Change `web.host` to `0.0.0.0` to expose externally - There is no authentication on any endpoint. **[SEC-3] Template update endpoint allows arbitrary file writes with no auth** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/templates.py`, lines 51-69 - **Issue:** `PUT /templates/{name}` modifies template content and writes to disk. No authentication, no input validation on template content (could contain script injection if templates are ever rendered as HTML). **[SEC-4] XSS in export error message** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/export.py`, line 50 - **Issue:** The error message uses `f'

ň»╝ňç║ňĄ▒Ŕ┤ą: {e}

'` without HTML escaping the exception message. If the exception contains user-controlled data (e.g., a filename), this is an XSS vector. ```python return HTMLResponse(f'

ň»╝ňç║ňĄ▒Ŕ┤ą: {e}

', status_code=500) ``` **[SEC-5] Message preview renders user-controlled content without escaping** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/messages.py`, line 51 - **Issue:** The preview HTML directly interpolates rendered template content: ```python preview_html = f'
{content}
' ``` If candidate names or template content contain HTML/JS, this is a stored XSS vector. The `content` variable comes from template rendering with database-sourced candidate data. **[SEC-6] Web service binds to `0.0.0.0` by default with no authentication** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/config/settings.json`, line 59 - **Issue:** The default host is `0.0.0.0`, exposing the service to the entire network. Combined with no authentication, anyone on the LAN can control the browser, send messages, modify settings, and access candidate data. The CLAUDE.md shows `127.0.0.1` as an alternative but it is not the default. --- ### 3. PERFORMANCE ISSUES **[PERF-1] Blocking synchronous HTTP calls in LLM scorer used from async pipeline** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/llm_scorer.py`, lines 145-155, 180-184 - **Issue:** `llm_score_candidate` uses synchronous `httpx.post()` (not `httpx.AsyncClient`). This function is called from `send_batch()` in `pipeline.py`, which runs inside the async TaskQueue worker. Each synchronous HTTP call blocks the entire asyncio event loop for up to 60 seconds (the timeout). For N candidates, this blocks the event loop for up to N*60 seconds, preventing all other async operations (health checks, SSE, web routes). ```python resp = httpx.post(...) # synchronous, blocks event loop ``` **[PERF-2] `check_ollama_available` uses synchronous HTTP in async context** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/llm_scorer.py`, line 18-19 - **Issue:** Same as above. `httpx.get()` is synchronous. Called from async `send_batch()` in pipeline. **[PERF-3] Dashboard route makes 4 separate COUNT queries** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/dashboard.py`, lines 21-29 - **Issue:** The dashboard makes 4 separate database queries (total_candidates, total_messages_sent, today_searches, today_messages) within the same session scope. These could be combined into a single query or at least the session is reused properly. Not critical but wasteful. **[PERF-4] `_batch_load_enrichment` queries all candidates without chunking** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/scoring.py`, lines 596-611 - **Issue:** Unlike `_save_candidates_to_db` in `search.py` which chunks queries by 500, this function uses `Candidate.boss_id.in_(boss_ids)` without any chunking. SQLite has a limit on the number of variables in a single query (default 999). If `boss_ids` has more than 999 entries, this will crash with `OperationalError: too many SQL variables`. --- ### 4. ARCHITECTURE ISSUES **[ARCH-1] Module-level singleton `ChromeProcessManager` instantiated at import time** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/app.py`, line 19 - **Issue:** `chrome_manager = ChromeProcessManager()` is created at module import time. Its `__init__` calls `get_config()` which instantiates the global Config singleton. This means importing `src.web.app` triggers config loading as a side effect. This causes issues in testing and when config files are not available. **[ARCH-2] Global mutable module state without thread safety** - **Files:** Multiple files use global mutable singletons: - `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/config.py` lines 88-95 (`_config_instance`) - `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/rate_limiter.py` lines 259-266 (`_rate_limiter`) - `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/core/session.py` lines 148-156 (`_session_manager`) - `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/models/database.py` lines 188-196 (`_db_manager`) - **Issue:** None of these use locks. While the web service is typically single-process, uvicorn can run with multiple workers, and the singletons are not process-safe. More importantly, `get_browser_manager()` at `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/core/browser.py` line 311 uses a global without a lock, though the BrowserManager class itself uses `_init_lock`. The `get_browser_manager()` function could create multiple instances under concurrent access. **[ARCH-3] `search.py` route bypasses TaskQueue for browser operations** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/search.py`, lines 58-70 - **Issue:** The search route directly calls `search_candidates()` without going through the TaskQueue. This means a search can run concurrently with a pipeline task, both accessing the same CDP browser simultaneously. The TaskQueue was designed to ensure serial browser access, but search bypasses it. Similarly, `/candidates/{boss_id}/extract` at candidates.py line 130 and `/messages/send` at messages.py line 95 also bypass the queue. **[ARCH-4] `auth/status` and `auth/check` routes call browser operations directly** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/auth.py`, lines 14-35 - **Issue:** These routes start the browser and navigate to a page (`check_login_status` navigates to a URL). This bypasses the TaskQueue and can conflict with pipeline operations. If a pipeline is running, the auth check will navigate the shared page away from whatever the pipeline was doing. --- ### 5. CODE QUALITY ISSUES **[QUAL-1] Dead `import sys` in `message.py:_type_and_send_message`** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/message.py`, line 463 - **Issue:** `import sys` is used inside the function body but `sys` is already imported at the module level (line 145). This shadowing is harmless but cluttered. **[QUAL-2] Inconsistent error handling: some web routes escape exceptions, others do not** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/export.py`, line 50 - Other routes use `html.escape(str(e))` but export route does not. **[QUAL-3] `_maybe_distraction_pause` uses a global counter without lock** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/delays.py`, line 38-39 - **Issue:** `global _action_count; _action_count += 1` is not atomic. In concurrent async contexts (unlikely to be truly concurrent due to GIL but still a code smell). **[QUAL-4] `Candidate.profile_url` read from expunged ORM object** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/profile.py`, lines 204-207 - **Issue:** The code opens a `session_scope`, reads `candidate.profile_url`, then exits the context manager (which closes the session). The `profile_url` value is captured correctly inside the `with` block, but if the candidate had a lazily-loaded relationship, accessing it after session close would fail. Currently safe but fragile pattern - the function accesses `candidate.profile_url` correctly within the session scope. **[QUAL-5] `_legacy_score` can crash on `None` title** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/scoring.py`, line 495 - **Issue:** `re.search(pattern, title, re.IGNORECASE)` is called where `title` could be empty string (handled by the `if title` at line 480) but the `rd_bonus_patterns` loop at line 494 uses `title` which was already set to empty string. Actually this is safe because of the guard at line 480: `if search_keywords and title:` only adds keyword score when title is truthy, but the rd_bonus loop at 494 has NO guard - it will call `re.search(pattern, title, ...)` with `title=""` which works fine (returns None). toolUseResult.prompt:
Thoroughly scan the boss-assistant codebase for engineering defects. This is a Python project for browser automation (recruiting assistant for BossšŤ┤Ŕüś).

Focus on these categories:
1. **Bugs and logic errors** ÔÇö incorrect logic, missing error handling, race conditions, type mismatches
2. **Security issues** ÔÇö hardcoded credentials, injection risks, unsafe deserialization, missing input validation
3. **Performance** ÔÇö N+1 queries, unnecessary repeated computation, blocking calls in async code
4. **Architecture** ÔÇö circular dependencies, unclear responsibility boundaries, improper patterns
5. **Code quality** ÔÇö inconsistent naming, code duplication, overly broad exception handling

Key areas to scan:
- src/web/ (FastAPI routes, services, task_queue, templates)
- src/core/ (browser manager, anti-detection, session)
- src/modules/ (auth, search, profile, message, export)
- src/services/pipeline.py
- src/models/database.py
- src/utils/ (config, rate_limiter, delays, retry, validators, captcha, checkpoint, scoring, logger)
- src/exceptions/errors.py
- config/ (settings.json, selectors.json, message_templates.json)
- run_web.py, boss_flow.py
- tests/

Known already-fixed issues from previous reviews (DO NOT report these):
- datetime naive/aware was already unified to timezone-aware
- N+1 queries in search and export were already fixed
- URL encoding in search was already fixed
- CancelledError handler was already added to pipeline routes
- XSS escaping was already added to HTMLResponse error messages
- captcha.py exception logging was already added
- task_queue.py double-done events were already fixed

Look for NEW issues that haven't been found before. Be thorough - check every file in src/ and tests/. Report findings with exact file paths and line numbers.

content.text:
I have now thoroughly read every file in the codebase. Let me compile my findings. --- ## Engineering Defects Report After a thorough scan of every file in the codebase, here are the NEW defects found, organized by category. --- ### 1. BUGS AND LOGIC ERRORS **[BUG-1] Race condition: `_last_action_time` ignores naive/aware datetime mixing in rate limiter comparison** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/rate_limiter.py`, line 170-172 - **Issue:** `_get_last_action_time` queries `func.max(ActionLog.timestamp)` from SQLite. SQLite stores timestamps as strings and SQLAlchemy returns naive datetimes from SQLite by default. The result `last_action` is compared against `now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)` (timezone-aware). This `(now - last_action).total_seconds()` will raise `TypeError: can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes` on Python 3. - **Relevant code:** ```python # line 123 now = datetime.now(timezone.utc) # aware # line 169 last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type=action_type) # naive from SQLite # line 171 elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds() # TypeError! ``` - **Note:** The same issue exists at lines 127-128 and 148-149 where `_count_actions` is called with an aware `since` datetime but the database `ActionLog.timestamp` column stores naive values from `_utcnow()` - the default function returns aware, but existing data or data from direct `ActionLog(timestamp=...)` may vary. However, the `_get_last_action_time` path is the most dangerous because it returns a raw SQLAlchemy scalar and directly subtracts. **[BUG-2] `send_message` non-interactive mode never passes `interactive=False` to chat flow** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/message.py`, lines 95-101 - **Issue:** When `send_message()` is called from the web route via `messages.py:send_msg`, it does not pass `interactive=False`. The function defaults `interactive=True` (line 81). When `interactive=True` and `sys.stdin.isatty()` returns `False` (common in FastAPI/uvicorn), `confirm` becomes `False` (line 154), and the message is cancelled. This means web single-message sends via `/messages/send` always get cancelled. - **Relevant code:** ```python # src/web/routes/messages.py line 95 result = await send_message( boss_id=boss_id, template_name=template_name, variables=variables, preview_only=False, browser=browser, # missing: interactive=False ) ``` **[BUG-3] Checkbox form values not parsed correctly for `skip_messaged` and `dry_run`** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/pipeline.py`, lines 66-67 - **Issue:** HTML checkboxes submit `"true"` as a string when checked, and are absent from the form data when unchecked. `Form(True)` and `Form(False)` defaults do not behave as expected: - For `skip_messaged: bool = Form(True)`: When unchecked, the browser sends nothing for this field, so the default `True` is used. Correct behavior. - When checked, the browser sends `"true"` (string). FastAPI Form coerces this to `True`. Correct. - For `dry_run: bool = Form(False)`: When checked, sends `"true"`, coerces to `True`. Correct. - BUT: `include_messages` in export uses the same pattern and the issue is that `Form(True)` for `include_messages` actually has the same unchecked-means-absent problem. When unchecked, the value defaults to `True` per `Form(True)`, making it impossible to export without messages. - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/export.py`, line 24 ```python include_messages: bool = Form(True), # unchecked checkbox = absent = True (impossible to disable) ``` **[BUG-4] `_scroll_search_results` JavaScript has a syntax error in multi-line template string** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/search.py`, lines 452-453 - **Issue:** The JavaScript string passed to `page.evaluate()` uses a Python triple-quoted string, but line 452-453 has a line break in the middle of a JS string literal inside `querySelectorAll()`: ```python const containers = document.querySelectorAll( '[class*="recommend-list"], [class*="geek-list"], [class*="search-result"], [class*="candidate-list"], [class*="card-list"], [class*="scroll-container"]' ); ``` This is a multi-line JavaScript string literal. In JavaScript, a string literal enclosed in single quotes cannot span lines. When `page.evaluate()` sends this to Chrome, it will produce a SyntaxError. The fix would be to concatenate the strings or use template literals. **[BUG-5] Export datetime formatting silently drops NaT values** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/export.py`, line 220 - **Issue:** `.dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M').replace('NaT', '')` - `strftime` applied to a Series produces NaT for missing values, but `.replace('NaT', '')` uses pandas string `replace` which only replaces the literal substring `'NaT'` within each cell string, not cells that ARE the string 'NaT'. The correct approach would be `.dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M').fillna('')` or `.where(df[col].notna(), '')`. **[BUG-6] `boss_flow_search.py` has absurd sleep duration** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/boss_flow_search.py`, line 15 - **Issue:** `await asyncio.sleep(1770796716.7)` -- this is ~56 years. The recorder captured an actual timestamp delta here instead of a reasonable delay. If anyone runs this script, it hangs forever. --- ### 2. SECURITY ISSUES **[SEC-1] SQL injection via ILIKE with unsanitized user input** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/candidates.py`, lines 28-33 - **Issue:** The search parameter `q` is interpolated into a LIKE pattern with `f"%{q}%"`. If the user provides `%` or `_` characters, they become LIKE wildcards and alter the query. This is LIKE injection. While it is not full SQL injection (parameterized queries are used), it allows the user to craft patterns that match unintended rows or cause performance degradation. ```python like = f"%{q}%" query = query.filter( Candidate.name.ilike(like) | Candidate.title.ilike(like) | Candidate.company.ilike(like) ) ``` **[SEC-2] Settings endpoint allows arbitrary JSON writes with no validation or authentication** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/settings.py`, lines 28-49 - **Issue:** The `POST /settings` endpoint accepts any JSON and writes it directly to `settings.json` without any validation. An attacker (or accidental user action) could: - Delete all settings (empty JSON) - Add malicious entries - Overwrite rate limits to zero (disabling all protections) - Change `web.host` to `0.0.0.0` to expose externally - There is no authentication on any endpoint. **[SEC-3] Template update endpoint allows arbitrary file writes with no auth** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/templates.py`, lines 51-69 - **Issue:** `PUT /templates/{name}` modifies template content and writes to disk. No authentication, no input validation on template content (could contain script injection if templates are ever rendered as HTML). **[SEC-4] XSS in export error message** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/export.py`, line 50 - **Issue:** The error message uses `f'

ň»╝ňç║ňĄ▒Ŕ┤ą: {e}

'` without HTML escaping the exception message. If the exception contains user-controlled data (e.g., a filename), this is an XSS vector. ```python return HTMLResponse(f'

ň»╝ňç║ňĄ▒Ŕ┤ą: {e}

', status_code=500) ``` **[SEC-5] Message preview renders user-controlled content without escaping** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/messages.py`, line 51 - **Issue:** The preview HTML directly interpolates rendered template content: ```python preview_html = f'
{content}
' ``` If candidate names or template content contain HTML/JS, this is a stored XSS vector. The `content` variable comes from template rendering with database-sourced candidate data. **[SEC-6] Web service binds to `0.0.0.0` by default with no authentication** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/config/settings.json`, line 59 - **Issue:** The default host is `0.0.0.0`, exposing the service to the entire network. Combined with no authentication, anyone on the LAN can control the browser, send messages, modify settings, and access candidate data. The CLAUDE.md shows `127.0.0.1` as an alternative but it is not the default. --- ### 3. PERFORMANCE ISSUES **[PERF-1] Blocking synchronous HTTP calls in LLM scorer used from async pipeline** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/llm_scorer.py`, lines 145-155, 180-184 - **Issue:** `llm_score_candidate` uses synchronous `httpx.post()` (not `httpx.AsyncClient`). This function is called from `send_batch()` in `pipeline.py`, which runs inside the async TaskQueue worker. Each synchronous HTTP call blocks the entire asyncio event loop for up to 60 seconds (the timeout). For N candidates, this blocks the event loop for up to N*60 seconds, preventing all other async operations (health checks, SSE, web routes). ```python resp = httpx.post(...) # synchronous, blocks event loop ``` **[PERF-2] `check_ollama_available` uses synchronous HTTP in async context** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/llm_scorer.py`, line 18-19 - **Issue:** Same as above. `httpx.get()` is synchronous. Called from async `send_batch()` in pipeline. **[PERF-3] Dashboard route makes 4 separate COUNT queries** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/dashboard.py`, lines 21-29 - **Issue:** The dashboard makes 4 separate database queries (total_candidates, total_messages_sent, today_searches, today_messages) within the same session scope. These could be combined into a single query or at least the session is reused properly. Not critical but wasteful. **[PERF-4] `_batch_load_enrichment` queries all candidates without chunking** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/scoring.py`, lines 596-611 - **Issue:** Unlike `_save_candidates_to_db` in `search.py` which chunks queries by 500, this function uses `Candidate.boss_id.in_(boss_ids)` without any chunking. SQLite has a limit on the number of variables in a single query (default 999). If `boss_ids` has more than 999 entries, this will crash with `OperationalError: too many SQL variables`. --- ### 4. ARCHITECTURE ISSUES **[ARCH-1] Module-level singleton `ChromeProcessManager` instantiated at import time** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/app.py`, line 19 - **Issue:** `chrome_manager = ChromeProcessManager()` is created at module import time. Its `__init__` calls `get_config()` which instantiates the global Config singleton. This means importing `src.web.app` triggers config loading as a side effect. This causes issues in testing and when config files are not available. **[ARCH-2] Global mutable module state without thread safety** - **Files:** Multiple files use global mutable singletons: - `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/config.py` lines 88-95 (`_config_instance`) - `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/rate_limiter.py` lines 259-266 (`_rate_limiter`) - `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/core/session.py` lines 148-156 (`_session_manager`) - `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/models/database.py` lines 188-196 (`_db_manager`) - **Issue:** None of these use locks. While the web service is typically single-process, uvicorn can run with multiple workers, and the singletons are not process-safe. More importantly, `get_browser_manager()` at `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/core/browser.py` line 311 uses a global without a lock, though the BrowserManager class itself uses `_init_lock`. The `get_browser_manager()` function could create multiple instances under concurrent access. **[ARCH-3] `search.py` route bypasses TaskQueue for browser operations** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/search.py`, lines 58-70 - **Issue:** The search route directly calls `search_candidates()` without going through the TaskQueue. This means a search can run concurrently with a pipeline task, both accessing the same CDP browser simultaneously. The TaskQueue was designed to ensure serial browser access, but search bypasses it. Similarly, `/candidates/{boss_id}/extract` at candidates.py line 130 and `/messages/send` at messages.py line 95 also bypass the queue. **[ARCH-4] `auth/status` and `auth/check` routes call browser operations directly** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/auth.py`, lines 14-35 - **Issue:** These routes start the browser and navigate to a page (`check_login_status` navigates to a URL). This bypasses the TaskQueue and can conflict with pipeline operations. If a pipeline is running, the auth check will navigate the shared page away from whatever the pipeline was doing. --- ### 5. CODE QUALITY ISSUES **[QUAL-1] Dead `import sys` in `message.py:_type_and_send_message`** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/message.py`, line 463 - **Issue:** `import sys` is used inside the function body but `sys` is already imported at the module level (line 145). This shadowing is harmless but cluttered. **[QUAL-2] Inconsistent error handling: some web routes escape exceptions, others do not** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/web/routes/export.py`, line 50 - Other routes use `html.escape(str(e))` but export route does not. **[QUAL-3] `_maybe_distraction_pause` uses a global counter without lock** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/delays.py`, line 38-39 - **Issue:** `global _action_count; _action_count += 1` is not atomic. In concurrent async contexts (unlikely to be truly concurrent due to GIL but still a code smell). **[QUAL-4] `Candidate.profile_url` read from expunged ORM object** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/modules/profile.py`, lines 204-207 - **Issue:** The code opens a `session_scope`, reads `candidate.profile_url`, then exits the context manager (which closes the session). The `profile_url` value is captured correctly inside the `with` block, but if the candidate had a lazily-loaded relationship, accessing it after session close would fail. Currently safe but fragile pattern - the function accesses `candidate.profile_url` correctly within the session scope. **[QUAL-5] `_legacy_score` can crash on `None` title** - **File:** `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/boss-assistant/src/utils/scoring.py`, line 495 - **Issue:** `re.search(pattern, title, re.IGNORECASE)` is called where `title` could be empty string (handled by the `if title` at line 480) but the `rd_bonus_patterns` loop at line 494 uses `title` which was already set to empty string. Connection errors, configuration errors, import errors -- all get silently swallowed.     47Ôćĺ

    67Ôćĺš╗╝ňÉłŔÇâŔÖĹŠŐÇŔâŻňî╣ÚůŹň║ŽŃÇüš╗ĆÚ¬îšŤŞňů│ŠÇžŃÇüňşŽňÄćŔâîŠÖ»ŃÇüŔüîńŞÜňĆĹň▒ĽŔŻĘŔ┐╣´╝îš╗Öňç║0-100ňłćšÜäŔ»äňłćŃÇé
    68Ôćĺ
    69ÔćĺŔ»ĚńŞąŠá╝Šîëń╗ąńŞőJSONŠá╝ň╝ĆŔ┐öňŤ×´╝łńŞŹŔŽüňîůňÉźňůÂń╗ľňćůň«╣´╝ë:
    70Ôćĺ{{"score": <0-100šÜäŠĽ┤ŠĽ░>, "explanation": "<ńŞÇňĆąŔ»ŁńŞşŠľçŔ»┤ŠśÄ>"}}"""
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺdef _parse_llm_response(text: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    74Ôćĺ    """Parse LLM response with multiple fallback strategies."""
    75Ôćĺ    if not text:
    76Ôćĺ        return None
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ    # Strategy 1: Direct JSON parse
    79Ôćĺ    try:
    80Ôćĺ        result = json.loads(text.strip())
    81Ôćĺ        if isinstance(result, dict) and "score" in result:
    82Ôćĺ            return _validate_result(result)
    83Ôćĺ    except json.JSONDecodeError:
    84Ôćĺ        pass
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ    # Strategy 2: Extract from code block
    87Ôćĺ    code_block = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*(\{.*?\})\s*```", text, re.DOTALL)
    88Ôćĺ    if code_block:
    89Ôćĺ        try:
    90Ôćĺ            result = json.loads(code_block.group(1))
    91Ôćĺ            if isinstance(result, dict) and "score" in result:
    92Ôćĺ                return _validate_result(result)
    93Ôćĺ        except json.JSONDecodeError:
    94Ôćĺ            pass
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ    # Strategy 3: Find JSON object in text
    97Ôćĺ    json_match = re.search(r"\{[^{}]*\"score\"\s*:\s*\d+[^{}]*\}", text)
    98Ôćĺ    if json_match:
    99Ôćĺ        try:
   100Ôćĺ            result = json.loads(json_match.group(0))
   101Ôćĺ            return _validate_result(result)
   102Ôćĺ        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   103Ôćĺ            pass
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ    # Strategy 4: Regex extract score number
   106Ôćĺ    score_match = re.search(r"[\"']?score[\"']?\s*[:´╝Ü]\s*(\d+)", text)
   107Ôćĺ    if score_match:
   108Ôćĺ        score = int(score_match.group(1))
   109Ôćĺ        explanation_match = re.search(r"[\"']?explanation[\"']?\s*[:´╝Ü]\s*[\"']([^\"']+)[\"']", text)
   110Ôćĺ        explanation = explanation_match.group(1) if explanation_match else ""
   111Ôćĺ        return _validate_result({"score": score, "explanation": explanation})
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ    logger.warning(f"Failed to parse LLM response: {text[:200]}")
   114Ôćĺ    return None
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺdef _validate_result(result: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   118Ôćĺ    """Validate and clamp the score."""
   119Ôćĺ    try:
   120Ôćĺ        score = int(result.get("score", -1))
   121Ôćĺ        if 0 <= score <= 100:
   122Ôćĺ            return {
   123Ôćĺ                "score": score,
   124Ôćĺ                "explanation": str(result.get("explanation", "")),
   125Ôćĺ            }
   126Ôćĺ    except (ValueError, TypeError):
   127Ôćĺ        pass
   128Ôćĺ    return None
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺdef llm_score_candidate(
   132Ôćĺ    candidate_info: Dict[str, Any],
   133Ôćĺ    jd_config: Dict[str, Any],
   134Ôćĺ    endpoint: str = "http://127.0.0.1:11434",
   135Ôćĺ    model: str = "qwen2.5:7b",
   136Ôćĺ    timeout_seconds: int = 60,
   137Ôćĺ) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   138Ôćĺ    """Score a single candidate using Ollama LLM.
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ    Returns:
   141Ôćĺ        {"score": 0-100, "explanation": "..."} or None on failure.
   142Ôćĺ    """
   143Ôćĺ    prompt = _build_scoring_prompt(candidate_info, jd_config)
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ    try:
   146Ôćĺ        resp = httpx.post(
   147Ôćĺ            f"{endpoint.rstrip('/')}/api/chat",
   148Ôćĺ            json={
   149Ôćĺ                "model": model,
   150Ôćĺ                "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
   151Ôćĺ                "stream": False,
   152Ôćĺ                "options": {"temperature": 0.1},
   153Ôćĺ            },
   154Ôćĺ            timeout=timeout_seconds,
   155Ôćĺ        )
   156Ôćĺ        resp.raise_for_status()
   157Ôćĺ        data = resp.json()
   158Ôćĺ        content = data.get("message", {}).get("content", "")
   159Ôćĺ        result = _parse_llm_response(content)
   160Ôćĺ        if result:
   161Ôćĺ            logger.debug(f"LLM scored {candidate_info.get('name', '?')}: {result['score']}")
   162Ôćĺ        return result
   163Ôćĺ    except Exception as e:
   164Ôćĺ        logger.warning(f"LLM scoring failed for {candidate_info.get('name', '?')}: {e}")
   165Ôćĺ        return None
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺdef llm_score_candidates_batch(
   169Ôćĺ    candidates_info: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   170Ôćĺ    jd_config: Dict[str, Any],
   171Ôćĺ    endpoint: str = "http://127.0.0.1:11434",
   172Ôćĺ    model: str = "qwen2.5:7b",
   173Ôćĺ    timeout_seconds: int = 60,
   174Ôćĺ) -> List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]:
   175Ôćĺ    """Score multiple candidates sequentially.
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ    Returns:
   178Ôćĺ        List of results, same length as input. None for failed entries.
   179Ôćĺ    """
   180Ôćĺ    results = []
   181Ôćĺ    for i, info in enumerate(candidates_info):
   182Ôćĺ        logger.info(f"LLM scoring candidate {i+1}/{len(candidates_info)}: {info.get('name', '?')}")
   183Ôćĺ        result = llm_score_candidate(info, jd_config, endpoint, model, timeout_seconds)
   184Ôćĺ        results.append(result)
   185Ôćĺ    return results
   186Ôćĺ

"""Settings routes ÔÇö view and edit configuration.""" import json from pathlib import Path from fastapi import APIRouter, Request, Form from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse, RedirectResponse from src.web.app import templates from src.utils.config import get_config router = APIRouter() @router.get("/", response_class=HTMLResponse) async def settings_page(request: Request): """Render settings page with current configuration.""" config = get_config() return templates.TemplateResponse("settings.html", { "request": request, "settings": config._settings, "settings_json": json.dumps(config._settings, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), }) @router.post("/") async def save_settings(request: Request, settings_json: str = Form(...)): """Save settings to disk.""" config = get_config() try: new_settings = json.loads(settings_json) except json.JSONDecodeError as e: return HTMLResponse( f'

JSON Šá╝ň╝ĆÚöÖŔ»»: {e}

', status_code=400, ) # Update in-memory config._settings = new_settings # Persist to disk settings_path = Path(config.config_dir) / "settings.json" with open(settings_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f: json.dump(new_settings, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) f.write('\n') return RedirectResponse(url="/settings", status_code=303)

"""Auto-generated by recorder.py"""

import asyncio
from patchright.async_api import async_playwright


async def replay(cdp_url='http://localhost:9222'):
    p = await async_playwright().start()
    browser = await p.chromium.connect_over_cdp(cdp_url)
    context = browser.contexts[0]
    page = context.pages[0] if context.pages else await context.new_page()

    await asyncio.sleep(3445.7)
    await page.locator('text="ŠÉťš┤ó"').click()
    await asyncio.sleep(1770796716.7)
    await page.goto('https://www.zhipin.com/web/chat/search', wait_until='domcontentloaded')
    await page.goto('https://www.zhipin.com/web/chat/search', wait_until='domcontentloaded')
    await asyncio.sleep(39.4)
    await page.evaluate('window.scrollTo(0, 0)')
    await asyncio.sleep(1.4)
    await page.evaluate('window.scrollTo(0, 0)')
    await asyncio.sleep(1.3)
    await page.evaluate('window.scrollTo(0, 0)')
    await asyncio.sleep(3.2)
    await page.locator('text="ŠÉťš┤óšĽůŔüŐňŹí(3/10)"').click()
    await asyncio.sleep(3.9)
    await page.locator('text="Ščąšťőň»╣Ŕ»Ł"').click()
    await page.evaluate('window.scrollTo(0, 0)')
    await asyncio.sleep(6.8)
    await page.locator('div.conversation-bd-content > div.bosschat-conversation-wrap.chat-wrap > div.chat-global-top > div.iboss.iboss-close').click()
    await asyncio.sleep(3.7)
    await page.locator('div.dialog-wrap.active > div.boss-popup__wrapper.boss-dialog > div.boss-popup__close > i.icon-close').click()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.run(replay())

async def send_message(
    boss_id: str,
    template_name: str,
    variables: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
    preview_only: bool = False,
    interactive: bool = True,
    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
) -> bool:
    """
    Send a templated message to a candidate on BossšŤ┤Ŕüś.

    Args:
        boss_id: Candidate Boss ID
        template_name: Name of the message template
        variables: Template variable overrides
        preview_only: If True, only preview the message without sending
        browser: Browser manager instance

    Returns:
        True if message sent successfully
    """
    if browser is None:
        browser = get_browser_manager()

    page = browser.page
    if not page:
        page = await browser.start()

    config = get_config()
    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
    db_manager = get_db_manager()

    # Load template
    template = config.get_template(template_name)

    # Merge variables
    final_vars = dict(variables or {})

    # Try to fill candidate info from database
    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
        candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(boss_id=boss_id).first()
        if candidate:
            if 'name' not in final_vars and candidate.name:
                final_vars['name'] = candidate.name
            if 'company' not in final_vars and candidate.company:
                final_vars['company'] = candidate.company
            if 'position' not in final_vars and candidate.title:
                final_vars['position'] = candidate.title

    # Validate variables
    validate_template_variables(template, final_vars)

    # Render message
    message_content = render_template(template, final_vars)

    # Preview
    console.print(Panel(
        f"[bold]To:[/bold] {boss_id}\n"
        f"[bold]Template:[/bold] {template_name}\n"
        f"[bold]Message:[/bold]\n{message_content}",
        title="Message Preview",
        border_style="cyan"
    ))

    if preview_only:
        console.print("[dim]Preview only ÔÇö message not sent.[/dim]")
        return False

    # Confirm send ÔÇö skip confirmation in non-interactive mode (Web)
    import sys
    if not interactive:
        confirm = True
    elif sys.stdin.isatty():
        confirm = questionary.confirm(
            "Send this message?",
            default=False,
        ).ask()
    else:
        confirm = False

    if not confirm:
        console.print("[yellow]Message cancelled.[/yellow]")
        # Record cancellation
        _save_message_to_db(boss_id, template_name, message_content, 'cancelled', db_manager)

def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
        if not self._pruned:
            self.prune_old_entries()
            self._pruned = True

        self._refresh_throttle()
        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)

        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)

        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()

                if oldest_in_window:
                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
                else:
                    wait_seconds = 3600

            raise RateLimitExceededError(
                action_type=action_type,
                wait_time=wait_seconds,
                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
            )

        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
        if daily_count >= per_day:
            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()

                if oldest_in_window:
                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
                else:
                    wait_seconds = 86400

            raise RateLimitExceededError(
                action_type=action_type,
                wait_time=wait_seconds,
                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
            )

        last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type=action_type)
        if last_action:
            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
            if elapsed < min_interval:
                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
                raise RateLimitExceededError(
                    action_type=action_type,
                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
                    message=f"Minimum interval not met for {action_type}. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
                )

        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")

    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
            action_log = ActionLog(

Now let me verify the findings and check a few more files.

Let me verify:

1. **BUG-2 (send_message missing interactive=False)**: CONFIRMED. Looking at `messages.py:95-101`, `send_message` is called without `interactive=False`. And in `message.py:81`, `interactive` defaults to `True`. At line 146-154, when `interactive=True` and not a TTY (which is the case for uvicorn/FastAPI), `confirm = False`, and the message gets cancelled. This is a real bug.

2. **BUG-4 (JS syntax error in multi-line string)**: Let me look more carefully. The Python string is `"""..."""` which preserves newlines. 

Looking at the JavaScript selector string, it's using single quotes that span across multiple lines, which would definitely cause a syntax error since JavaScript string literals can't break across lines like that. I see the issue nowÔÇöthat JavaScript string with the newline inside single quotes would definitely cause a syntax error. Looking at the security vulnerabilities, I've confirmed the XSS issues in the export and message preview functions where user content isn't being properly escaped, and there's also a problem with the checkbox logic for the include_messages parameter.

The synchronous HTTP call in the LLM scorer is a performance bottleneck, and there's that bizarre 56-year sleep somewhere in the boss flow search. For the datetime mismatch issue, I need to trace through how the database stores and retrieves timestampsÔÇöthe rate limiter is comparing what should be aware datetimes, but I'm checking whether SQLite is actually preserving that timezone information correctly.

Looking at the SSE connections in the pipeline progress HTML, there's definitely duplication happeningÔÇöHTMX sets up one connection on line 3, and then the script manually creates another EventSource on line 69, so that's two separate connections running simultaneously. The LIKE injection concern is relatively minor since it's parameterized, though user input with wildcards could still cause unexpected query behavior. The authentication issues are more architecturalÔÇöthis is designed as a local tool, so those are design trade-offs rather than critical vulnerabilities.

The real performance concern is in `scoring.py` around line 600, where the boss_ids query doesn't chunk the input, and SQLite's default limit of 999 variables could cause crashes with larger datasets. Let me check the `_get_last_action_time` method to confirm the BUG-1 issue.

def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Candidate(boss_id='{self.boss_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"


class Message(Base):
    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""

    __tablename__ = 'messages'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    candidate_boss_id = Column(String(50), ForeignKey('candidates.boss_id'), index=True)
    template_name = Column(String(100))
    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    sent_at = Column(DateTime)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)

    # Relationships
    candidate = relationship("Candidate", back_populates="messages")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_boss_id}', status='{self.status}')>"

                | Candidate.company.ilike(like)
            )

        total = query.count()
        candidates = query.offset((page - 1) * per_page).limit(per_page).all()

class SearchHistory(Base):
    """Search history model - tracks all searches performed."""

    __tablename__ = 'search_history'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    criteria = Column(JSON)
    results_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    executed_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, index=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SearchHistory(id={self.id}, results={self.results_count}, date='{self.executed_at}')>"


class ActionLog(Base):
    """Action log model - tracks all actions for rate limiting."""

    __tablename__ = 'action_logs'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    action_type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)
    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, nullable=False)
    details = Column(JSON)

    __table_args__ = (
        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
    )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"


class PipelineRun(Base):
    """Pipeline run history ÔÇö tracks each pipeline execution."""

    __tablename__ = 'pipeline_runs'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)

content.thinking:
Now let me check the BUG-1 (naive/aware datetime) more carefully:

The `ActionLog.timestamp` has `default=_utcnow` where `_utcnow` is `datetime.now(timezone.utc)`. So when inserting, it stores aware datetimes. But SQLite stores datetimes as strings (ISO format). When reading back via SQLAlchemy, SQLite returns them as naive datetimes (without timezone info). So when `_get_last_action_time` returns a value, it would be a naive datetime. And then `now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)` is aware. The subtraction `(now - last_action).total_seconds()` would indeed raise `TypeError`.
 
I need to verify how SQLAlchemy's DateTime column actually handles timezone preservation with SQLite. The key is whether the `timezone` parameter on the DateTime column affects how values are parsed when read backÔÇöif it's set to `True`, SQLAlchemy should preserve timezone info, but if it's `False` or unset, the datetimes come back naive. by default, no timezone awareness
- `Column(DateTime(timezone=True))` - timezone-aware

Looking at `ActionLog.timestamp = Column(DateTime, ...)` - no `timezone=True`, so SQLAlchemy treats it as naive.

When stored, `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` gives `2026-03-03 10:00:00+00:00`. SQLite stores this as the string. When read back, depending on the SQLAlchemy dialect, it may strip the timezone info.

With SQLite's default dialect, `DateTime` columns store datetimes as ISO format strings, which may preserve timezone offsets on write but the behavior on read depends on the driverÔÇöthe standard `sqlite3` module might or might not return timezone-aware values. This ambiguity creates a real problem in the code where `func.max()` queries and datetime arithmetic at lines 130-136 and 150-156 could fail if one operand is naive and the other is aware, though whether this actually manifests depends on the runtime environment.

For BUG-5, I'm checking the pandas code more carefully. The `.dt.strftime()` method returns a Series of strings, and calling `.replace('NaT', '')` on that Series uses pandas' `Series.replace()` method, which correctly replaces any cell containing the string 'NaT' with an empty stringÔÇöso this isn't actually a bug.

Actually, waitÔÇöI need to reconsider what `strftime()` returns for NaT values. In pandas, when you call `dt.strftime()` on a Series containing NaT values, those NaT values become NaN (not the string 'NaT'), so the `.replace('NaT', '')` wouldn't catch them. The proper fix would be to use `.fillna('')` instead.

So BUG-5 is partially correctÔÇöthe `.replace('NaT', '')` doesn't actually work as intended because NaT converts to NaN in the strftime result, and `.replace()` won't match NaN values. The correct approach is `.fillna('')`.

Now let me verify the JavaScript syntax error claim by checking that code snippet...

Checking JavaScript syntax... I've confirmed that BUG-4 is realÔÇöthe newline character in the JavaScript string literal will cause a syntax error when the code runs. Now I'm filtering the bugs by severity to present to the user: the most critical ones are the message cancellation issue, the scroll functionality breaking due to that JS error, and the potential event loop blocking in the LLM scorer, with the XSS vulnerability in error exports as a secondary concern.

Beyond those, there's a range of medium and lower-priority issues spanning XSS in message previews, an uncheckable checkbox, datetime comparison bugs, duplicate SSE connections, and a concerning default bind to 0.0.0.0 without authentication, plus performance problems with batch enrichment that could crash on large datasets. The remaining issues are mostly minorÔÇömalformed datetime exports, an absurdly long sleep timer, SQL injection risks in search, and some endpoint security gaps.

I'm narrowing my focus to the most actionable bugs rather than architectural concerns, so I'll prioritize the critical and medium-severity items for the report while noting which previous findings still apply.

Changing `Form(True)` to `Form(False)` means unchecked = False (correct), but checked = "true" string which coerces to True (correct). But wait - the checkbox needs to actually send the field value when checked. Let me check the export template to see how the checkbox is rendered.

Actually, looking at this more carefully - the fix for `include_messages` is to change the default to `Form(False)`. This way:
- Unchecked: field absent Ôćĺ default False Ôťô


- Checked: field "true" Ôćĺ True Ôťô

The real issue is that the checkbox should be checked by default in the HTML template while keeping the Form default as False, so unchecked sends nothing (False) and checked sends the value (True). Let me verify the template has the proper checked attribute.

def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
            if action_type:
                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
            last_time = query.scalar()
            return last_time

    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
        if not self._pruned:
            self.prune_old_entries()
            self._pruned = True

        self._refresh_throttle()
        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)

        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)

        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()

                if oldest_in_window:
                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
                else:
                    wait_seconds = 3600

            raise RateLimitExceededError(
                action_type=action_type,
                wait_time=wait_seconds,
                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
            )

        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
        if daily_count >= per_day:
            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()

                if oldest_in_window:
                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
                else:
                    wait_seconds = 86400

            raise RateLimitExceededError(
                action_type=action_type,
                wait_time=wait_seconds,
                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
            )

        last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type=action_type)
        if last_action:
            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
            if elapsed < min_interval:
                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
                raise RateLimitExceededError(
                    action_type=action_type,
                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
                    message=f"Minimum interval not met for {action_type}. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
                )

        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")

content.thinking:
There are 3 places where SQLite-returned datetime values are compared with aware `now`:
1. Line 136: `oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)` then `wait_until - now`
2. Line 157: `oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)` then `wait_until - now`
3. Line 171: `now - last_action`

The cleanest fix is a helper function that ensures any datetime from SQLite gets timezone info added.
