1Ôćĺ"""Authentication module for BossšŤ┤Ŕüś login.
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺIn CDP mode, the user logs in manually in their own Chrome before running
     4ÔćĺCLI commands. This module verifies login status and provides guidance
     5Ôćĺif the user is not logged in.
     6Ôćĺ"""
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺimport asyncio
     9Ôćĺfrom typing import Optional
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺfrom patchright.async_api import Page
    12Ôćĺfrom loguru import logger
    13Ôćĺfrom rich.console import Console
    14Ôćĺfrom rich.panel import Panel
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺfrom src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17Ôćĺfrom src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    18Ôćĺfrom src.utils.retry import retry_navigation
    19Ôćĺfrom src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺconsole = Console()
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺasync def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    25Ôćĺ    """
    26Ôćĺ    Verify login status in CDP-connected Chrome.
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ    In CDP mode, the user is expected to already be logged in to BossšŤ┤Ŕüś.
    29Ôćĺ    If not, instructions are printed for manual login in the browser.
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ    Args:
    32Ôćĺ        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ    Returns:
    35Ôćĺ        True if logged in
    36Ôćĺ    """
    37Ôćĺ    if browser is None:
    38Ôćĺ        browser = get_browser_manager()
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ    # Connect to Chrome via CDP
    41Ôćĺ    page = await browser.start()
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ    # Check if already logged in
    44Ôćĺ    if await check_login_status(page):
    45Ôćĺ        console.print("[green]Already logged in to BossšŤ┤Ŕüś![/green]")
    46Ôćĺ        # Save session as backup
    47Ôćĺ        await browser.save_session()
    48Ôćĺ        return True
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ    # Not logged in ÔÇö guide the user
    51Ôćĺ    console.print(Panel(
    52Ôćĺ        "[bold]Not Logged In[/bold]\n\n"
    53Ôćĺ        "Please log in to BossšŤ┤Ŕüś in your Chrome browser:\n"
    54Ôćĺ        "1. Go to https://www.zhipin.com/web/user/?ka=header-login in Chrome\n"
    55Ôćĺ        "2. Scan QR code with BossšŤ┤Ŕüś app, OR\n"
    56Ôćĺ        "3. Enter phone number and verification code\n\n"
    57Ôćĺ        "[dim]Then wait here, login will be detected automatically.[/dim]",
    58Ôćĺ        title="BossšŤ┤Ŕüś Login",
    59Ôćĺ        border_style="cyan"
    60Ôćĺ    ))
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ    # Poll for login (user may log in while we wait)
    63Ôćĺ    max_wait_seconds = 300  # 5 minutes timeout
    64Ôćĺ    poll_interval = 3
    65Ôćĺ    elapsed = 0
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ    console.print("[dim]Waiting for login... (timeout: 5 minutes)[/dim]")
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ    while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
    70Ôćĺ        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
    71Ôćĺ        elapsed += poll_interval
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ        # Check for captcha
    74Ôćĺ        await check_captcha(page)
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ        if await check_login_status(page):
    77Ôćĺ            console.print("[green]Login successful![/green]")
    78Ôćĺ            await browser.save_session()
    79Ôćĺ            console.print("[green]Session saved as backup[/green]")
    80Ôćĺ            return True
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ    console.print("[red]Login timeout. Please log in manually and try again.[/red]")
    83Ôćĺ    return False
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺasync def check_login_status(page: Page) -> bool:
    87Ôćĺ    """
    88Ôćĺ    Check if user is currently logged in to BossšŤ┤Ŕüś by navigating to a
    89Ôćĺ    protected page and observing whether BossšŤ┤Ŕüś redirects to the login page.
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ    Args:
    92Ôćĺ        page: Playwright page instance
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ    Returns:
    95Ôćĺ        True if logged in (no redirect to login page)
    96Ôćĺ    """
    97Ôćĺ    try:
    98Ôćĺ        # Navigate to a protected page that requires authentication.
    99Ôćĺ        # The Boss search page reliably triggers server-side auth checks.
   100Ôćĺ        protected_url = "https://www.zhipin.com/web/chat/search"
   101Ôćĺ        await retry_navigation(page, protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ        # Wait for any redirects to settle
   104Ôćĺ        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ        # Check 1: URL-based detection - did we get redirected to login?
   107Ôćĺ        final_url = page.url
   108Ôćĺ        if 'login' in final_url.lower() or 'signin' in final_url.lower():
   109Ôćĺ            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=redirected_to_login (url={final_url})")
   110Ôćĺ            return False
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ        # Check 2: Page content detection - look for login form elements
   113Ôćĺ        is_login_page = await page.evaluate("""
   114Ôćĺ            () => {
   115Ôćĺ                const bodyText = document.body ? document.body.innerText : '';
   116Ôćĺ                const loginIndicators = [
   117Ôćĺ                    'ŠČóŔ┐ÄšÖ╗ňŻĽ',
   118Ôćĺ                    'ŠëźšáüšÖ╗ňŻĽ',
   119Ôćĺ                    'ň»ćšáüšÖ╗ňŻĽ',
   120Ôćĺ                    'ščşń┐íšÖ╗ňŻĽ',
   121Ôćĺ                    'Ŕ»ĚŔżôňůąŠëőŠť║ňĆĚ',
   122Ôćĺ                    'ŔÄĚňĆľÚ¬îŔ»üšáü',
   123Ôćĺ                    'ňż«ń┐íšÖ╗ňŻĽ',
   124Ôćĺ                    'šÖ╗ňŻĽ/Š│Ęňćî'
   125Ôćĺ                ];
   126Ôćĺ                for (const indicator of loginIndicators) {
   127Ôćĺ                    if (bodyText.includes(indicator)) {
   128Ôćĺ                        return { is_login: true, matched: indicator };
   129Ôćĺ                    }
   130Ôćĺ                }
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ                // Also check for login form elements in DOM
   133Ôćĺ                const loginForm = document.querySelector(
   134Ôćĺ                    'input[type="tel"], input[placeholder*="ŠëőŠť║"], ' +
   135Ôćĺ                    '[class*="qrcode"], [class*="scan-login"], ' +
   136Ôćĺ                    '[class*="login-form"], [class*="sign-form"]'
   137Ôćĺ                );
   138Ôćĺ                if (loginForm) {
   139Ôćĺ                    return { is_login: true, matched: 'login_form_element' };
   140Ôćĺ                }
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ                return { is_login: false, matched: null };
   143Ôćĺ            }
   144Ôćĺ        """)
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ        if is_login_page.get('is_login', False):
   147Ôćĺ            detail = is_login_page.get('matched', 'unknown')
   148Ôćĺ            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=login_page_detected ({detail})")
   149Ôćĺ            return False
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ        logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=True, detail=protected_page_accessible (url={final_url})")
   152Ôćĺ        return True
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ    except Exception as e:
   155Ôćĺ        logger.error(f"Error checking login status: {e}")
   156Ôćĺ        return False
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺasync def ensure_logged_in(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
   160Ôćĺ    """
   161Ôćĺ    Ensure user is logged in, prompting for login if needed.
   162Ôćĺ
   163Ôćĺ    Args:
   164Ôćĺ        browser: Browser manager instance
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ    Returns:
   167Ôćĺ        True if logged in (existing or new login)
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ    Raises:
   170Ôćĺ        SessionExpiredError: If login fails
   171Ôćĺ    """
   172Ôćĺ    if browser is None:
   173Ôćĺ        browser = get_browser_manager()
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ    # Connect to Chrome via CDP if not already connected
   176Ôćĺ    if not browser.is_running:
   177Ôćĺ        await browser.start()
   178Ôćĺ
   179Ôćĺ    # Check login status
   180Ôćĺ    if browser.page:
   181Ôćĺ        if await check_login_status(browser.page):
   182Ôćĺ            return True
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺ    # Not logged in ÔÇö guide user
   185Ôćĺ    console.print("[yellow]Not logged in. Please log in to BossšŤ┤Ŕüś in your Chrome browser.[/yellow]")
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ    if await login(browser):
   188Ôćĺ        return True
   189Ôćĺ
   190Ôćĺ    raise SessionExpiredError("Not logged in to BossšŤ┤Ŕüś. Please log in manually in Chrome and try again.")
   191Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ"""Export module for exporting candidate data.
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺSupports Excel (.xlsx) and CSV formats with optional
     4Ôćĺmessage history inclusion.
     5Ôćĺ"""
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺfrom datetime import datetime
     8Ôćĺfrom pathlib import Path
     9Ôćĺfrom typing import Optional, Literal, List, Dict, Any
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺimport pandas as pd
    12Ôćĺfrom openpyxl import Workbook
    13Ôćĺfrom openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment, Border, Side
    14Ôćĺfrom openpyxl.utils.dataframe import dataframe_to_rows
    15Ôćĺfrom loguru import logger
    16Ôćĺfrom rich.console import Console
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺfrom src.utils.config import get_config
    19Ôćĺfrom src.models.database import Candidate, Message, get_db_manager
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺconsole = Console()
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺdef export_candidates(
    25Ôćĺ    output_format: Literal["excel", "xlsx", "csv"] = "excel",
    26Ôćĺ    output_path: Optional[str] = None,
    27Ôćĺ    include_messages: bool = True
    28Ôćĺ) -> str:
    29Ôćĺ    """
    30Ôćĺ    Export candidate data to Excel or CSV.
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ    Args:
    33Ôćĺ        output_format: Export format ('excel', 'xlsx', or 'csv')
    34Ôćĺ        output_path: Output file path (auto-generated if None)
    35Ôćĺ        include_messages: Include message history
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ    Returns:
    38Ôćĺ        Path to exported file
    39Ôćĺ    """
    40Ôćĺ    config = get_config()
    41Ôćĺ    db_manager = get_db_manager()
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ    if output_format == "xlsx":
    44Ôćĺ        output_format = "excel"
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ    if output_path is None:
    47Ôćĺ        export_dir = Path(config.get('data.export_dir', 'data/exports'))
    48Ôćĺ        export_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    49Ôćĺ        timestamp = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')
    50Ôćĺ        extension = 'xlsx' if output_format == 'excel' else 'csv'
    51Ôćĺ        output_path = str(export_dir / f"candidates_{timestamp}.{extension}")
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ    Path(output_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ    candidates_data = _get_candidates_data(db_manager, include_messages)
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ    if not candidates_data:
    58Ôćĺ        console.print("[yellow]No candidates to export[/yellow]")
    59Ôćĺ        return ""
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ    df = _create_dataframe(candidates_data)
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ    if output_format == "excel":
    64Ôćĺ        _export_to_excel(df, output_path)
    65Ôćĺ    else:
    66Ôćĺ        _export_to_csv(df, output_path)
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ    console.print(f"[green]Exported {len(candidates_data)} candidates to: {output_path}[/green]")
    69Ôćĺ    return output_path
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺdef _get_candidates_data(db_manager, include_messages: bool) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    73Ôćĺ    """Get candidate data from database."""
    74Ôćĺ    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
    75Ôćĺ        candidates = session.query(Candidate).all()
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ        data = []
    78Ôćĺ        for candidate in candidates:
    79Ôćĺ            record = {
    80Ôćĺ                'boss_id': candidate.boss_id,
    81Ôćĺ                'name': candidate.name,
    82Ôćĺ                'title': candidate.title,
    83Ôćĺ                'company': candidate.company,
    84Ôćĺ                'location': candidate.location,
    85Ôćĺ                'salary_expect': candidate.salary_expect,
    86Ôćĺ                'work_years': candidate.work_years,
    87Ôćĺ                'education_level': candidate.education_level,
    88Ôćĺ                'profile_url': candidate.profile_url,
    89Ôćĺ                'first_seen': candidate.first_seen,
    90Ôćĺ                'last_updated': candidate.last_updated,
    91Ôćĺ                'source_search': candidate.source_search,
    92Ôćĺ            }
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ            # Flatten education
    95Ôćĺ            education = candidate.education or []
    96Ôćĺ            if education:
    97Ôćĺ                latest_edu = education[0]
    98Ôćĺ                record['latest_education'] = _format_education(latest_edu)
    99Ôćĺ            else:
   100Ôćĺ                record['latest_education'] = ''
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ            # Flatten work experience
   103Ôćĺ            work_exp = candidate.work_experience or []
   104Ôćĺ            if work_exp:
   105Ôćĺ                latest_work = work_exp[0]
   106Ôćĺ                record['latest_experience'] = _format_work_experience(latest_work)
   107Ôćĺ            else:
   108Ôćĺ                record['latest_experience'] = ''
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ            # Format skills
   111Ôćĺ            skills = candidate.skills or []
   112Ôćĺ            record['skills'] = ', '.join(skills) if skills else ''
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ            data.append(record)
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ        # Batch-fetch message info to avoid N+1 queries
   117Ôćĺ        if include_messages and data:
   118Ôćĺ            from sqlalchemy import func
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ            boss_ids = [r['boss_id'] for r in data]
   121Ôćĺ            # Get message counts and latest message per candidate in bulk
   122Ôćĺ            msg_counts = {}
   123Ôćĺ            latest_msgs = {}
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ            # Count messages per candidate
   126Ôćĺ            count_rows = session.query(
   127Ôćĺ                Message.candidate_boss_id,
   128Ôćĺ                func.count(Message.id).label('cnt')
   129Ôćĺ            ).filter(
   130Ôćĺ                Message.candidate_boss_id.in_(boss_ids)
   131Ôćĺ            ).group_by(Message.candidate_boss_id).all()
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ            for row in count_rows:
   134Ôćĺ                msg_counts[row.candidate_boss_id] = row.cnt
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ            # Get latest message per candidate using a subquery
   137Ôćĺ            from sqlalchemy import desc
   138Ôćĺ            all_msgs = session.query(Message).filter(
   139Ôćĺ                Message.candidate_boss_id.in_(boss_ids)
   140Ôćĺ            ).order_by(Message.candidate_boss_id, desc(Message.created_at)).all()
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ            for msg in all_msgs:
   143Ôćĺ                if msg.candidate_boss_id not in latest_msgs:
   144Ôćĺ                    latest_msgs[msg.candidate_boss_id] = msg
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ            for record in data:
   147Ôćĺ                bid = record['boss_id']
   148Ôćĺ                record['message_count'] = msg_counts.get(bid, 0)
   149Ôćĺ                latest_msg = latest_msgs.get(bid)
   150Ôćĺ                if latest_msg:
   151Ôćĺ                    record['last_message_status'] = latest_msg.status
   152Ôćĺ                    record['last_message_date'] = latest_msg.sent_at or latest_msg.created_at
   153Ôćĺ                    record['last_message_template'] = latest_msg.template_name
   154Ôćĺ                else:
   155Ôćĺ                    record['last_message_status'] = ''
   156Ôćĺ                    record['last_message_date'] = None
   157Ôćĺ                    record['last_message_template'] = ''
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺ        return data
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺdef _format_education(edu: Dict) -> str:
   163Ôćĺ    """Format education entry as string."""
   164Ôćĺ    parts = []
   165Ôćĺ    if edu.get('school'):
   166Ôćĺ        parts.append(edu['school'])
   167Ôćĺ    if edu.get('degree'):
   168Ôćĺ        parts.append(edu['degree'])
   169Ôćĺ    if edu.get('major'):
   170Ôćĺ        parts.append(edu['major'])
   171Ôćĺ    return ' - '.join(parts) if parts else ''
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺdef _format_work_experience(work: Dict) -> str:
   175Ôćĺ    """Format work experience entry as string."""
   176Ôćĺ    parts = []
   177Ôćĺ    if work.get('title'):
   178Ôćĺ        parts.append(work['title'])
   179Ôćĺ    if work.get('company'):
   180Ôćĺ        parts.append(f"@ {work['company']}")
   181Ôćĺ    return ' '.join(parts) if parts else ''
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺdef _create_dataframe(data: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> pd.DataFrame:
   185Ôćĺ    """Create pandas DataFrame from candidate data."""
   186Ôćĺ    columns = [
   187Ôćĺ        'boss_id',
   188Ôćĺ        'name',
   189Ôćĺ        'title',
   190Ôćĺ        'company',
   191Ôćĺ        'location',
   192Ôćĺ        'salary_expect',
   193Ôćĺ        'work_years',
   194Ôćĺ        'education_level',
   195Ôćĺ        'latest_education',
   196Ôćĺ        'latest_experience',
   197Ôćĺ        'skills',
   198Ôćĺ        'profile_url',
   199Ôćĺ        'source_search',
   200Ôćĺ        'first_seen',
   201Ôćĺ        'last_updated',
   202Ôćĺ    ]
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ    if data and 'message_count' in data[0]:
   205Ôćĺ        columns.extend([
   206Ôćĺ            'message_count',
   207Ôćĺ            'last_message_status',
   208Ôćĺ            'last_message_date',
   209Ôćĺ            'last_message_template',
   210Ôćĺ        ])
   211Ôćĺ
   212Ôćĺ    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
   213Ôćĺ
   214Ôćĺ    existing_columns = [c for c in columns if c in df.columns]
   215Ôćĺ    df = df[existing_columns]
   216Ôćĺ
   217Ôćĺ    datetime_columns = ['first_seen', 'last_updated', 'last_message_date']
   218Ôćĺ    for col in datetime_columns:
   219Ôćĺ        if col in df.columns:
   220Ôćĺ            df[col] = pd.to_datetime(df[col]).dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M').fillna('')
   221Ôćĺ
   222Ôćĺ    return df
   223Ôćĺ
   224Ôćĺ
   225Ôćĺdef _export_to_excel(df: pd.DataFrame, output_path: str) -> None:
   226Ôćĺ    """Export DataFrame to Excel with formatting."""
   227Ôćĺ    wb = Workbook()
   228Ôćĺ    ws = wb.active
   229Ôćĺ    ws.title = "Candidates"
   230Ôćĺ
   231Ôćĺ    header_font = Font(bold=True, color="FFFFFF")
   232Ôćĺ    header_fill = PatternFill(start_color="4472C4", end_color="4472C4", fill_type="solid")
   233Ôćĺ    header_alignment = Alignment(horizontal="center", vertical="center", wrap_text=True)
   234Ôćĺ
   235Ôćĺ    border = Border(
   236Ôćĺ        left=Side(style='thin'),
   237Ôćĺ        right=Side(style='thin'),
   238Ôćĺ        top=Side(style='thin'),
   239Ôćĺ        bottom=Side(style='thin')
   240Ôćĺ    )
   241Ôćĺ
   242Ôćĺ    for r_idx, row in enumerate(dataframe_to_rows(df, index=False, header=True), 1):
   243Ôćĺ        for c_idx, value in enumerate(row, 1):
   244Ôćĺ            cell = ws.cell(row=r_idx, column=c_idx, value=value)
   245Ôćĺ            cell.border = border
   246Ôćĺ
   247Ôćĺ            if r_idx == 1:
   248Ôćĺ                cell.font = header_font
   249Ôćĺ                cell.fill = header_fill
   250Ôćĺ                cell.alignment = header_alignment
   251Ôćĺ            else:
   252Ôćĺ                cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center", wrap_text=True)
   253Ôćĺ
   254Ôćĺ    column_widths = {
   255Ôćĺ        'A': 20,  # boss_id
   256Ôćĺ        'B': 12,  # name
   257Ôćĺ        'C': 25,  # title
   258Ôćĺ        'D': 20,  # company
   259Ôćĺ        'E': 12,  # location
   260Ôćĺ        'F': 15,  # salary_expect
   261Ôćĺ        'G': 12,  # work_years
   262Ôćĺ        'H': 12,  # education_level
   263Ôćĺ        'I': 30,  # latest_education
   264Ôćĺ        'J': 30,  # latest_experience
   265Ôćĺ        'K': 40,  # skills
   266Ôćĺ        'L': 40,  # profile_url
   267Ôćĺ        'M': 20,  # source_search
   268Ôćĺ        'N': 18,  # first_seen
   269Ôćĺ        'O': 18,  # last_updated
   270Ôćĺ        'P': 12,  # message_count
   271Ôćĺ        'Q': 15,  # last_message_status
   272Ôćĺ        'R': 18,  # last_message_date
   273Ôćĺ        'S': 15,  # last_message_template
   274Ôćĺ    }
   275Ôćĺ
   276Ôćĺ    for col, width in column_widths.items():
   277Ôćĺ        ws.column_dimensions[col].width = width
   278Ôćĺ
   279Ôćĺ    ws.freeze_panes = 'A2'
   280Ôćĺ    ws.auto_filter.ref = ws.dimensions
   281Ôćĺ
   282Ôćĺ    wb.save(output_path)
   283Ôćĺ    logger.info(f"Excel file saved to: {output_path}")
   284Ôćĺ
   285Ôćĺ
   286Ôćĺdef _export_to_csv(df: pd.DataFrame, output_path: str) -> None:
   287Ôćĺ    """Export DataFrame to CSV."""
   288Ôćĺ    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False, encoding='utf-8-sig')
   289Ôćĺ    logger.info(f"CSV file saved to: {output_path}")
   290Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ"""Profile extraction module for BossšŤ┤Ŕüś.
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺExtracts detailed candidate profile information from BossšŤ┤Ŕüś.
     4ÔćĺProfile details are shown as a floating overlay (ŠÁ«ň▒é) on the search page,
     5Ôćĺnot as a separate page. The extraction JS also supports standalone profile
     6Ôćĺpages as a fallback.
     7Ôćĺ"""
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺfrom typing import Optional, Dict, Any
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺfrom loguru import logger
    12Ôćĺfrom rich.console import Console
    13Ôćĺfrom rich.panel import Panel
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺfrom src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    16Ôćĺfrom src.utils.delays import async_random_delay
    17Ôćĺfrom src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter, async_check_rate_limit
    18Ôćĺfrom src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    19Ôćĺfrom src.utils.retry import retry_navigation
    20Ôćĺfrom src.models.database import Candidate, get_db_manager
    21Ôćĺfrom src.exceptions.errors import ExtractionError
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺconsole = Console()
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ# JavaScript extraction function for BossšŤ┤Ŕüś profile pages
    27ÔćĺEXTRACT_PROFILE_JS = """
    28Ôćĺ() => {
    29Ôćĺ    const result = {
    30Ôćĺ        name: '',
    31Ôćĺ        title: '',
    32Ôćĺ        company: '',
    33Ôćĺ        location: '',
    34Ôćĺ        salary_expect: '',
    35Ôćĺ        work_years: '',
    36Ôćĺ        education_level: '',
    37Ôćĺ        basic_info: {},
    38Ôćĺ        education: [],
    39Ôćĺ        work_experience: [],
    40Ôćĺ        skills: []
    41Ôćĺ    };
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ    // Extract basic info from the profile header area
    44Ôćĺ    const nameEl = document.querySelector(
    45Ôćĺ        '[class*="geek-name"], [class*="name"], h1[class*="name"], .name'
    46Ôćĺ    );
    47Ôćĺ    result.name = nameEl ? nameEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ    const titleEl = document.querySelector(
    50Ôćĺ        '[class*="expect-position"], [class*="title"], [class*="position"]'
    51Ôćĺ    );
    52Ôćĺ    result.title = titleEl ? titleEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ    const companyEl = document.querySelector(
    55Ôćĺ        '[class*="company-name"], [class*="company"]'
    56Ôćĺ    );
    57Ôćĺ    result.company = companyEl ? companyEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ    const locationEl = document.querySelector(
    60Ôćĺ        '[class*="expect-area"], [class*="location"], [class*="city"]'
    61Ôćĺ    );
    62Ôćĺ    result.location = locationEl ? locationEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ    const salaryEl = document.querySelector(
    65Ôćĺ        '[class*="expect-salary"], [class*="salary"]'
    66Ôćĺ    );
    67Ôćĺ    result.salary_expect = salaryEl ? salaryEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ    // Extract info tags (work years, education, age, etc.)
    70Ôćĺ    const tagEls = document.querySelectorAll(
    71Ôćĺ        '[class*="info-tag"], [class*="base-info"] span, [class*="geek-tags"] span, ' +
    72Ôćĺ        '[class*="tag-item"], [class*="basic-info"] span'
    73Ôćĺ    );
    74Ôćĺ    const tags = Array.from(tagEls).map(el => el.innerText.trim()).filter(t => t);
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ    for (const tag of tags) {
    77Ôćĺ        if (tag.includes('ň╣┤') && (tag.includes('š╗ĆÚ¬î') || /^\\d/.test(tag))) {
    78Ôćĺ            result.work_years = tag;
    79Ôćĺ            result.basic_info.work_years = tag;
    80Ôćĺ        } else if (['ňŹÜňúź', 'šíĽňúź', 'ŠťČšžĹ', 'ňĄžńŞô', 'ńŞşńŞô', 'ÚźśńŞş'].some(d => tag.includes(d))) {
    81Ôćĺ            result.education_level = tag;
    82Ôćĺ            result.basic_info.education = tag;
    83Ôćĺ        } else if (tag.includes('ň▓ü')) {
    84Ôćĺ            result.basic_info.age = tag;
    85Ôćĺ        } else if (tag.includes('Š▒éŔüî') || tag.includes('šŐÂŠÇü')) {
    86Ôćĺ            result.basic_info.job_status = tag;
    87Ôćĺ        }
    88Ôćĺ    }
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ    // Extract education details
    91Ôćĺ    const eduSection = document.querySelector(
    92Ôćĺ        '[class*="resume-education"], [class*="education-list"], [class*="edu-experience"]'
    93Ôćĺ    );
    94Ôćĺ    if (eduSection) {
    95Ôćĺ        const eduItems = eduSection.querySelectorAll(
    96Ôćĺ            '[class*="resume-item"], [class*="edu-item"], [class*="item"], li'
    97Ôćĺ        );
    98Ôćĺ        for (const item of eduItems) {
    99Ôćĺ            const text = item.innerText.trim();
   100Ôćĺ            if (!text) continue;
   101Ôćĺ            const lines = text.split('\\n').filter(l => l.trim());
   102Ôćĺ            const edu = {};
   103Ôćĺ            if (lines.length >= 1) edu.school = lines[0].trim();
   104Ôćĺ            if (lines.length >= 2) edu.degree = lines[1].trim();
   105Ôćĺ            if (lines.length >= 3) edu.major = lines[2].trim();
   106Ôćĺ            if (lines.length >= 4) edu.period = lines[3].trim();
   107Ôćĺ            result.education.push(edu);
   108Ôćĺ        }
   109Ôćĺ    }
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ    // Extract work experience
   112Ôćĺ    const workSection = document.querySelector(
   113Ôćĺ        '[class*="resume-work"], [class*="work-list"], [class*="work-experience"]'
   114Ôćĺ    );
   115Ôćĺ    if (workSection) {
   116Ôćĺ        const workItems = workSection.querySelectorAll(
   117Ôćĺ            '[class*="resume-item"], [class*="work-item"], [class*="item"], li'
   118Ôćĺ        );
   119Ôćĺ        for (const item of workItems) {
   120Ôćĺ            const text = item.innerText.trim();
   121Ôćĺ            if (!text) continue;
   122Ôćĺ            const lines = text.split('\\n').filter(l => l.trim());
   123Ôćĺ            const work = {};
   124Ôćĺ            if (lines.length >= 1) work.company = lines[0].trim();
   125Ôćĺ            if (lines.length >= 2) work.title = lines[1].trim();
   126Ôćĺ            if (lines.length >= 3) work.period = lines[2].trim();
   127Ôćĺ            if (lines.length >= 4) work.description = lines.slice(3).join(' ').trim();
   128Ôćĺ            result.work_experience.push(work);
   129Ôćĺ        }
   130Ôćĺ    }
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ    // Extract project experience (BossšŤ┤Ŕüś specific)
   133Ôćĺ    const projectSection = document.querySelector(
   134Ôćĺ        '[class*="resume-project"], [class*="project-list"]'
   135Ôćĺ    );
   136Ôćĺ    if (projectSection) {
   137Ôćĺ        result.basic_info.has_project = true;
   138Ôćĺ    }
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ    // Extract skills
   141Ôćĺ    const skillEls = document.querySelectorAll(
   142Ôćĺ        '[class*="skill-tag"], [class*="skill-item"], [class*="tag-item"], ' +
   143Ôćĺ        '[class*="skill"] span'
   144Ôćĺ    );
   145Ôćĺ    const skills = new Set();
   146Ôćĺ    for (const el of skillEls) {
   147Ôćĺ        const text = el.innerText.trim();
   148Ôćĺ        if (text && text.length < 30) {
   149Ôćĺ            skills.add(text);
   150Ôćĺ        }
   151Ôćĺ    }
   152Ôćĺ    result.skills = Array.from(skills);
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ    // Extract advantages/self description
   155Ôćĺ    const advantageEl = document.querySelector(
   156Ôćĺ        '[class*="resume-advantage"], [class*="self-desc"], [class*="geek-advantage"]'
   157Ôćĺ    );
   158Ôćĺ    if (advantageEl) {
   159Ôćĺ        result.basic_info.self_description = advantageEl.innerText.trim();
   160Ôćĺ    }
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ    return result;
   163Ôćĺ}
   164Ôćĺ"""
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺasync def extract_profile(
   168Ôćĺ    boss_id: str,
   169Ôćĺ    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
   170Ôćĺ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   171Ôćĺ    """
   172Ôćĺ    Extract detailed profile for a candidate.
   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ    Args:
   175Ôćĺ        boss_id: BossšŤ┤Ŕüś candidate ID (e.g., "zp_xxx")
   176Ôćĺ        browser: Browser manager instance
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ    Returns:
   179Ôćĺ        Dictionary with profile data
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ    Raises:
   182Ôćĺ        ExtractionError: If profile extraction fails
   183Ôćĺ    """
   184Ôćĺ    if browser is None:
   185Ôćĺ        browser = get_browser_manager()
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ    page = browser.page
   188Ôćĺ    if not page:
   189Ôćĺ        page = await browser.start()
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   192Ôćĺ    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   193Ôćĺ
   194Ôćĺ    # Rate limit check
   195Ôćĺ    await async_check_rate_limit('view_profile')
   196Ôćĺ
   197Ôćĺ    # BossšŤ┤Ŕüś shows profile as a floating overlay (ŠÁ«ň▒é) on the search page.
   198Ôćĺ    # Strategy 1: Try to extract from an already-open overlay on the current page.
   199Ôćĺ    # Strategy 2: Navigate to profile URL as a fallback.
   200Ôćĺ    encrypt_id = boss_id.replace('zp_', '') if boss_id.startswith('zp_') else boss_id
   201Ôćĺ
   202Ôćĺ    # First check if we have a profile URL in the database
   203Ôćĺ    profile_url = None
   204Ôćĺ    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   205Ôćĺ        candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(boss_id=boss_id).first()
   206Ôćĺ        if candidate and candidate.profile_url:
   207Ôćĺ            profile_url = candidate.profile_url
   208Ôćĺ
   209Ôćĺ    if not profile_url:
   210Ôćĺ        profile_url = f"https://www.zhipin.com/web/geek/{encrypt_id}"
   211Ôćĺ
   212Ôćĺ    try:
   213Ôćĺ        # Strategy 1: Check if a profile overlay is already open on the current page
   214Ôćĺ        overlay_present = await page.evaluate("""
   215Ôćĺ            () => {
   216Ôćĺ                const overlay = document.querySelector(
   217Ôćĺ                    '[class*="dialog-wrap"].active, [class*="boss-popup__wrapper"],
   218Ôćĺ                     [class*="geek-detail"], [class*="resume-dialog"], [class*="profile-panel"]'
   219Ôćĺ                );
   220Ôćĺ                return overlay !== null;
   221Ôćĺ            }
   222Ôćĺ        """)
   223Ôćĺ
   224Ôćĺ        if not overlay_present:
   225Ôćĺ            # Strategy 2: Navigate to profile page
   226Ôćĺ            await retry_navigation(page, profile_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   227Ôćĺ            await async_random_delay('page_load')
   228Ôćĺ
   229Ôćĺ            # Check for captcha
   230Ôćĺ            await check_captcha(page, report_to_rate_limiter=True)
   231Ôćĺ
   232Ôćĺ            # Wait for profile content to load
   233Ôćĺ            await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
   234Ôćĺ
   235Ôćĺ        # Extract profile data via JavaScript (works on both overlay and standalone page)
   236Ôćĺ        profile_data = await page.evaluate(EXTRACT_PROFILE_JS)
   237Ôćĺ
   238Ôćĺ        if not profile_data or not profile_data.get('name'):
   239Ôćĺ            logger.warning(f"Could not extract profile for {boss_id}")
   240Ôćĺ            raise ExtractionError(f"Failed to extract profile data for {boss_id}")
   241Ôćĺ
   242Ôćĺ        # Log action
   243Ôćĺ        rate_limiter.log_action('view_profile', {'boss_id': boss_id})
   244Ôćĺ
   245Ôćĺ        # Update database
   246Ôćĺ        _update_profile_in_db(boss_id, profile_data, db_manager)
   247Ôćĺ
   248Ôćĺ        # Display profile
   249Ôćĺ        _display_profile(boss_id, profile_data)
   250Ôćĺ
   251Ôćĺ        return profile_data
   252Ôćĺ
   253Ôćĺ    except ExtractionError:
   254Ôćĺ        raise
   255Ôćĺ    except Exception as e:
   256Ôćĺ        logger.error(f"Error extracting profile for {boss_id}: {e}")
   257Ôćĺ        raise ExtractionError(f"Failed to extract profile for {boss_id}: {e}")
   258Ôćĺ
   259Ôćĺ
   260Ôćĺdef _update_profile_in_db(boss_id: str, profile_data: Dict, db_manager) -> None:
   261Ôćĺ    """Update candidate profile in the database."""
   262Ôćĺ    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   263Ôćĺ        candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(boss_id=boss_id).first()
   264Ôćĺ
   265Ôćĺ        if not candidate:
   266Ôćĺ            # Create new candidate entry
   267Ôćĺ            candidate = Candidate(boss_id=boss_id)
   268Ôćĺ            session.add(candidate)
   269Ôćĺ
   270Ôćĺ        # Update fields
   271Ôćĺ        candidate.name = profile_data.get('name') or candidate.name
   272Ôćĺ        candidate.title = profile_data.get('title') or candidate.title
   273Ôćĺ        candidate.company = profile_data.get('company') or candidate.company
   274Ôćĺ        candidate.location = profile_data.get('location') or candidate.location
   275Ôćĺ        candidate.salary_expect = profile_data.get('salary_expect') or candidate.salary_expect
   276Ôćĺ        candidate.work_years = profile_data.get('work_years') or candidate.work_years
   277Ôćĺ        candidate.education_level = profile_data.get('education_level') or candidate.education_level
   278Ôćĺ        candidate.basic_info = profile_data.get('basic_info')
   279Ôćĺ        candidate.education = profile_data.get('education')
   280Ôćĺ        candidate.work_experience = profile_data.get('work_experience')
   281Ôćĺ        candidate.skills = profile_data.get('skills')
   282Ôćĺ
   283Ôćĺ
   284Ôćĺdef _display_profile(boss_id: str, profile_data: Dict) -> None:
   285Ôćĺ    """Display extracted profile in Rich format."""
   286Ôćĺ    name = profile_data.get('name', 'Unknown')
   287Ôćĺ    title = profile_data.get('title', '')
   288Ôćĺ    company = profile_data.get('company', '')
   289Ôćĺ    location = profile_data.get('location', '')
   290Ôćĺ    salary = profile_data.get('salary_expect', '')
   291Ôćĺ    work_years = profile_data.get('work_years', '')
   292Ôćĺ    education_level = profile_data.get('education_level', '')
   293Ôćĺ
   294Ôćĺ    # Header
   295Ôćĺ    header = f"[bold]{name}[/bold]"
   296Ôćĺ    if title:
   297Ôćĺ        header += f" - {title}"
   298Ôćĺ    if company:
   299Ôćĺ        header += f" @ {company}"
   300Ôćĺ
   301Ôćĺ    console.print(Panel(header, title=f"Profile: {boss_id}", border_style="green"))
   302Ôćĺ
   303Ôćĺ    # Basic info
   304Ôćĺ    info_parts = []
   305Ôćĺ    if location:
   306Ôćĺ        info_parts.append(f"Location: {location}")
   307Ôćĺ    if salary:
   308Ôćĺ        info_parts.append(f"Expected Salary: {salary}")
   309Ôćĺ    if work_years:
   310Ôćĺ        info_parts.append(f"Experience: {work_years}")
   311Ôćĺ    if education_level:
   312Ôćĺ        info_parts.append(f"Education: {education_level}")
   313Ôćĺ
   314Ôćĺ    if info_parts:
   315Ôćĺ        console.print("  " + " | ".join(info_parts))
   316Ôćĺ
   317Ôćĺ    # Education
   318Ôćĺ    education = profile_data.get('education', [])
   319Ôćĺ    if education:
   320Ôćĺ        console.print("\n  [cyan]Education:[/cyan]")
   321Ôćĺ        for edu in education:
   322Ôćĺ            parts = []
   323Ôćĺ            if edu.get('school'):
   324Ôćĺ                parts.append(edu['school'])
   325Ôćĺ            if edu.get('degree'):
   326Ôćĺ                parts.append(edu['degree'])
   327Ôćĺ            if edu.get('major'):
   328Ôćĺ                parts.append(edu['major'])
   329Ôćĺ            console.print(f"    - {' | '.join(parts)}")
   330Ôćĺ
   331Ôćĺ    # Work experience

1Ôćĺ"""Chrome process lifecycle manager for Web service.
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺManages starting, health-checking, and restarting the Chrome browser
     4Ôćĺprocess with CDP enabled. Used by the Web service lifespan to ensure
     5ÔćĺChrome is always available for browser automation tasks.
     6Ôćĺ"""
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺimport asyncio
     9Ôćĺimport shutil
    10Ôćĺimport signal
    11Ôćĺimport subprocess
    12Ôćĺimport sys
    13Ôćĺfrom pathlib import Path
    14Ôćĺfrom typing import Optional
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺimport httpx
    17Ôćĺfrom loguru import logger
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺfrom src.utils.config import get_config
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺclass ChromeProcessManager:
    23Ôćĺ    """Manages the Chrome browser process for the Web service.
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ    - Auto-starts Chrome in headed mode with --remote-debugging-port
    26Ôćĺ    - Periodically health-checks via CDP HTTP endpoint
    27Ôćĺ    - Auto-restarts Chrome if it crashes
    28Ôćĺ    - Gracefully terminates Chrome on shutdown
    29Ôćĺ    """
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ    def __init__(self):
    32Ôćĺ        self._config = get_config()
    33Ôćĺ        self._process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
    34Ôćĺ        self._health_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
    35Ôćĺ        self._running = False
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ    @property
    38Ôćĺ    def debug_port(self) -> int:
    39Ôćĺ        return self._config.get('web.chrome_debug_port', 9222)
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ    @property
    42Ôćĺ    def user_data_dir(self) -> str:
    43Ôćĺ        return self._config.get('web.chrome_user_data_dir', 'data/.chrome-profile')
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ    @property
    46Ôćĺ    def cdp_url(self) -> str:
    47Ôćĺ        return f'http://127.0.0.1:{self.debug_port}'
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ    @property
    50Ôćĺ    def is_chrome_running(self) -> bool:
    51Ôćĺ        return self._process is not None and self._process.poll() is None
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ    def _find_chrome_binary(self) -> str:
    54Ôćĺ        """Find Chrome binary on the current platform."""
    55Ôćĺ        if sys.platform == 'darwin':
    56Ôćĺ            candidates = [
    57Ôćĺ                '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome',
    58Ôćĺ                '/Applications/Chromium.app/Contents/MacOS/Chromium',
    59Ôćĺ            ]
    60Ôćĺ        elif sys.platform == 'win32':
    61Ôćĺ            candidates = [
    62Ôćĺ                r'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
    63Ôćĺ                r'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
    64Ôćĺ            ]
    65Ôćĺ        else:
    66Ôćĺ            candidates = [
    67Ôćĺ                'google-chrome',
    68Ôćĺ                'google-chrome-stable',
    69Ôćĺ                'chromium',
    70Ôćĺ                'chromium-browser',
    71Ôćĺ            ]
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ        for candidate in candidates:
    74Ôćĺ            if Path(candidate).exists() or shutil.which(candidate):
    75Ôćĺ                return candidate
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ        raise RuntimeError(
    78Ôćĺ            'Chrome binary not found. Install Google Chrome or set the path manually.'
    79Ôćĺ        )
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ    async def _check_cdp_health(self) -> bool:
    82Ôćĺ        """Check if Chrome CDP endpoint is responsive."""
    83Ôćĺ        try:
    84Ôćĺ            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
    85Ôćĺ                resp = await client.get(
    86Ôćĺ                    f'{self.cdp_url}/json/version',
    87Ôćĺ                    timeout=5.0,
    88Ôćĺ                )
    89Ôćĺ                return resp.status_code == 200
    90Ôćĺ        except Exception:
    91Ôćĺ            return False
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ    def _start_chrome_process(self) -> None:
    94Ôćĺ        """Start Chrome with CDP enabled."""
    95Ôćĺ        chrome_binary = self._find_chrome_binary()
    96Ôćĺ        user_data = Path(self.user_data_dir).resolve()
    97Ôćĺ        user_data.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ        args = [
   100Ôćĺ            chrome_binary,
   101Ôćĺ            f'--remote-debugging-port={self.debug_port}',
   102Ôćĺ            f'--user-data-dir={user_data}',
   103Ôćĺ            '--no-first-run',
   104Ôćĺ            '--no-default-browser-check',
   105Ôćĺ            '--disable-background-timer-throttling',
   106Ôćĺ            '--disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows',
   107Ôćĺ            '--disable-renderer-backgrounding',
   108Ôćĺ        ]
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ        logger.info(f'Starting Chrome: {" ".join(args[:3])}...')
   111Ôćĺ        self._process = subprocess.Popen(
   112Ôćĺ            args,
   113Ôćĺ            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   114Ôćĺ            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   115Ôćĺ        )
   116Ôćĺ        logger.info(f'Chrome started with PID {self._process.pid}')
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ    async def start(self) -> None:
   119Ôćĺ        """Start Chrome and begin health monitoring.
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ        If Chrome is already running on the debug port (e.g. user started
   122Ôćĺ        it manually), we skip launching a new process and just monitor.
   123Ôćĺ        """
   124Ôćĺ        self._running = True
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ        # Check if Chrome is already running on the port
   127Ôćĺ        if await self._check_cdp_health():
   128Ôćĺ            logger.info(f'Chrome already running on port {self.debug_port}, skipping launch')
   129Ôćĺ        elif self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
   130Ôćĺ            self._start_chrome_process()
   131Ôćĺ            # Wait for Chrome to be ready
   132Ôćĺ            for _ in range(30):
   133Ôćĺ                if await self._check_cdp_health():
   134Ôćĺ                    logger.info('Chrome CDP endpoint is ready')
   135Ôćĺ                    break
   136Ôćĺ                await asyncio.sleep(1)
   137Ôćĺ            else:
   138Ôćĺ                logger.error('Chrome failed to start within 30 seconds')
   139Ôćĺ        else:
   140Ôćĺ            logger.warning(
   141Ôćĺ                'Chrome auto-launch disabled and no Chrome detected on '
   142Ôćĺ                f'port {self.debug_port}'
   143Ôćĺ            )
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ        # Start health check loop
   146Ôćĺ        self._health_task = asyncio.create_task(self._health_loop())
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ    async def _health_loop(self) -> None:
   149Ôćĺ        """Periodically check Chrome health and restart if needed."""
   150Ôćĺ        while self._running:
   151Ôćĺ            await asyncio.sleep(10)
   152Ôćĺ            if not self._running:
   153Ôćĺ                break
   154Ôćĺ
   155Ôćĺ            healthy = await self._check_cdp_health()
   156Ôćĺ            if not healthy:
   157Ôćĺ                logger.warning('Chrome health check failed')
   158Ôćĺ                if self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
   159Ôćĺ                    await self._restart_chrome()
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ    async def _restart_chrome(self) -> None:
   162Ôćĺ        """Restart Chrome process and reset BrowserManager connection."""
   163Ôćĺ        logger.info('Restarting Chrome...')
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ        # Kill existing process if any
   166Ôćĺ        self._kill_process()
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ        await asyncio.sleep(2)
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ        # Start fresh
   171Ôćĺ        self._start_chrome_process()
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ        # Wait for ready
   174Ôćĺ        for _ in range(30):
   175Ôćĺ            if await self._check_cdp_health():
   176Ôćĺ                logger.info('Chrome restarted successfully')
   177Ôćĺ                break
   178Ôćĺ            await asyncio.sleep(1)
   179Ôćĺ        else:
   180Ôćĺ            logger.error('Chrome restart failed')
   181Ôćĺ            return
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ        # Reset BrowserManager singleton so it reconnects
   184Ôćĺ        from src.core.browser import BrowserManager
   185Ôćĺ        await BrowserManager.reset_instance()
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ    def _kill_process(self) -> None:
   188Ôćĺ        """Terminate the Chrome process."""
   189Ôćĺ        if self._process is None:
   190Ôćĺ            return
   191Ôćĺ
   192Ôćĺ        try:
   193Ôćĺ            self._process.send_signal(signal.SIGTERM)
   194Ôćĺ            try:
   195Ôćĺ                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
   196Ôćĺ            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   197Ôćĺ                self._process.kill()
   198Ôćĺ                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
   199Ôćĺ            logger.info('Chrome process terminated')
   200Ôćĺ        except Exception as e:
   201Ôćĺ            logger.warning(f'Error terminating Chrome: {e}')
   202Ôćĺ        finally:
   203Ôćĺ            self._process = None
   204Ôćĺ
   205Ôćĺ    async def stop(self) -> None:
   206Ôćĺ        """Stop health monitoring and terminate Chrome."""
   207Ôćĺ        self._running = False
   208Ôćĺ
   209Ôćĺ        if self._health_task and not self._health_task.done():
   210Ôćĺ            self._health_task.cancel()
   211Ôćĺ            try:
   212Ôćĺ                await self._health_task
   213Ôćĺ            except asyncio.CancelledError:
   214Ôćĺ                pass
   215Ôćĺ
   216Ôćĺ        self._kill_process()
   217Ôćĺ        logger.info('ChromeProcessManager stopped')
   218Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ"""Anti-detection module for browser automation.
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺImplements human-like interaction via Emunium + Patchright:
     4Ôćĺ- Human-like scrolling behavior (chunked, variable speed)
     5Ôćĺ- Human-like clicking via Emunium (natural mouse movement)
     6Ôćĺ- Human-like typing via Emunium (variable keystroke timing)
     7Ôćĺ
     8ÔćĺNote: Webdriver property hiding and navigator masking are handled
     9Ôćĺby Patchright automatically in CDP mode.
    10Ôćĺ"""
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺimport random
    13Ôćĺfrom typing import Optional
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺfrom patchright.async_api import Page
    16Ôćĺfrom emunium import EmuniumPlaywright
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺfrom src.exceptions.errors import SelectorNotFoundError
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺasync def human_scroll(page: Page, direction: str = 'down', distance: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
    22Ôćĺ    """
    23Ôćĺ    Perform human-like scrolling with variable speed.
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ    Args:
    26Ôćĺ        page: Playwright page instance
    27Ôćĺ        direction: 'up' or 'down'
    28Ôćĺ        distance: Scroll distance in pixels (random if None)
    29Ôćĺ    """
    30Ôćĺ    if direction not in ('up', 'down'):
    31Ôćĺ        raise ValueError(f"Invalid scroll direction: {direction!r}, expected 'up' or 'down'")
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ    if distance is None:
    34Ôćĺ        distance = random.randint(200, 600)
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ    if direction == 'up':
    37Ôćĺ        distance = -distance
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ    # Break scroll into smaller chunks
    40Ôćĺ    chunks = random.randint(3, 8)
    41Ôćĺ    chunk_size = distance // chunks
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ    for i in range(chunks):
    44Ôćĺ        actual_chunk = chunk_size + random.randint(-20, 20)
    45Ôćĺ        await page.mouse.wheel(0, actual_chunk)
    46Ôćĺ        delay = random.uniform(0.05, 0.15)
    47Ôćĺ        await page.wait_for_timeout(delay * 1000)
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ    await page.wait_for_timeout(random.uniform(0.2, 0.5) * 1000)
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺasync def human_click(page: Page, selector: str) -> None:
    53Ôćĺ    """
    54Ôćĺ    Click an element with human-like behavior using Emunium.
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ    Args:
    57Ôćĺ        page: Playwright page instance
    58Ôćĺ        selector: CSS selector for element to click
    59Ôćĺ    """
    60Ôćĺ    try:
    61Ôćĺ        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    62Ôćĺ    except Exception as e:
    63Ôćĺ        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    64Ôćĺ            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    65Ôćĺ        raise
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ    if element is None:
    68Ôćĺ        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
    71Ôćĺ    await emunium.click_at(element)
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺasync def human_type(page: Page, selector: str, text: str) -> None:
    75Ôćĺ    """
    76Ôćĺ    Type text with human-like timing using Emunium.
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ    Args:
    79Ôćĺ        page: Playwright page instance
    80Ôćĺ        selector: CSS selector for input element
    81Ôćĺ        text: Text to type
    82Ôćĺ    """
    83Ôćĺ    try:
    84Ôćĺ        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    85Ôćĺ    except Exception as e:
    86Ôćĺ        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    87Ôćĺ            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    88Ôćĺ        raise
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ    if element is None:
    91Ôćĺ        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
    94Ôćĺ    await emunium.type_at(element, text)
    95Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ"""Session management for persistent browser sessions.
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺHandles saving and loading browser sessions (cookies, localStorage)
     4Ôćĺto enable resuming without re-login.
     5Ôćĺ"""
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺimport json
     8Ôćĺimport os
     9Ôćĺimport stat
    10Ôćĺimport tempfile
    11Ôćĺfrom pathlib import Path
    12Ôćĺfrom datetime import datetime, timezone
    13Ôćĺfrom typing import Optional
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺfrom cryptography.fernet import Fernet
    16Ôćĺfrom patchright.async_api import BrowserContext
    17Ôćĺfrom loguru import logger
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺfrom src.utils.config import get_config
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺclass SessionManager:
    23Ôćĺ    """Manages browser session persistence."""
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ    def __init__(self, session_path: Optional[str] = None):
    26Ôćĺ        if session_path is None:
    27Ôćĺ            config = get_config()
    28Ôćĺ            session_path = config.get('data.session_path', 'data/.session/boss_session.json')
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ        self.session_path = Path(session_path)
    31Ôćĺ        self._key_path = self.session_path.parent / '.session_key'
    32Ôćĺ        self._fernet: Optional[Fernet] = None
    33Ôćĺ        self._ensure_session_dir()
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ    def _ensure_session_dir(self) -> None:
    36Ôćĺ        """Ensure session directory exists with restrictive permissions."""
    37Ôćĺ        session_dir = self.session_path.parent
    38Ôćĺ        session_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    39Ôćĺ        try:
    40Ôćĺ            os.chmod(session_dir, stat.S_IRWXU)
    41Ôćĺ        except OSError:
    42Ôćĺ            logger.warning("Could not set restrictive permissions on session directory")
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ    def _get_fernet(self) -> Fernet:
    45Ôćĺ        """Get or create Fernet encryption instance."""
    46Ôćĺ        if self._fernet is not None:
    47Ôćĺ            return self._fernet
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ        if self._key_path.exists():
    50Ôćĺ            key = self._key_path.read_bytes().strip()
    51Ôćĺ        else:
    52Ôćĺ            key = Fernet.generate_key()
    53Ôćĺ            fd = os.open(str(self._key_path), os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC, 0o600)
    54Ôćĺ            try:
    55Ôćĺ                os.write(fd, key)
    56Ôćĺ            finally:
    57Ôćĺ                os.close(fd)
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ        self._fernet = Fernet(key)
    60Ôćĺ        return self._fernet
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ    def _decrypt_session_data(self) -> dict:
    63Ôćĺ        """Decrypt and parse the stored session file."""
    64Ôćĺ        encrypted = self.session_path.read_bytes()
    65Ôćĺ        plaintext = self._get_fernet().decrypt(encrypted)
    66Ôćĺ        return json.loads(plaintext.decode('utf-8'))
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ    def _write_secure(self, path: Path, data: bytes) -> None:
    69Ôćĺ        """Atomically write data to file with owner-only permissions (0600)."""
    70Ôćĺ        tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=str(path.parent), suffix=".tmp")
    71Ôćĺ        try:
    72Ôćĺ            os.write(tmp_fd, data)
    73Ôćĺ            os.close(tmp_fd)
    74Ôćĺ            tmp_fd = -1
    75Ôćĺ            os.chmod(tmp_path, 0o600)
    76Ôćĺ            os.replace(tmp_path, str(path))
    77Ôćĺ        except BaseException:
    78Ôćĺ            if tmp_fd >= 0:
    79Ôćĺ                os.close(tmp_fd)
    80Ôćĺ            try:
    81Ôćĺ                os.unlink(tmp_path)
    82Ôćĺ            except OSError:
    83Ôćĺ                pass
    84Ôćĺ            raise
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ    def has_saved_session(self) -> bool:
    87Ôćĺ        return self.session_path.exists()
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ    async def save(self, context: BrowserContext) -> None:
    90Ôćĺ        try:
    91Ôćĺ            storage_state = await context.storage_state()
    92Ôćĺ            session_data = {
    93Ôćĺ                'saved_at': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    94Ôćĺ                'storage_state': storage_state
    95Ôćĺ            }
    96Ôćĺ            plaintext = json.dumps(session_data, ensure_ascii=False).encode('utf-8')
    97Ôćĺ            encrypted = self._get_fernet().encrypt(plaintext)
    98Ôćĺ            self._write_secure(self.session_path, encrypted)
    99Ôćĺ            logger.info(f"Session saved (encrypted) to {self.session_path}")
   100Ôćĺ        except Exception as e:
   101Ôćĺ            logger.error(f"Failed to save session: {e}")
   102Ôćĺ            raise
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ    async def clear(self) -> None:
   105Ôćĺ        """Clear saved session and encryption key."""
   106Ôćĺ        if self.session_path.exists():
   107Ôćĺ            self.session_path.unlink()
   108Ôćĺ            logger.info("Session cleared")
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ        state_path = self.session_path.parent / 'storage_state.json'
   111Ôćĺ        if state_path.exists():
   112Ôćĺ            state_path.unlink()
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ        if self._key_path.exists():
   115Ôćĺ            self._key_path.unlink()
   116Ôćĺ        self._fernet = None
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ    def get_session_info(self) -> Optional[dict]:
   119Ôćĺ        if not self.has_saved_session():
   120Ôćĺ            return None
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ        try:
   123Ôćĺ            session_data = self._decrypt_session_data()
   124Ôćĺ            saved_at = session_data.get('saved_at')
   125Ôćĺ            storage_state = session_data.get('storage_state', {})
   126Ôćĺ            cookies = storage_state.get('cookies', [])
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ            if saved_at:
   129Ôćĺ                saved_datetime = datetime.fromisoformat(saved_at)
   130Ôćĺ                if saved_datetime.tzinfo is None:
   131Ôćĺ                    saved_datetime = saved_datetime.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   132Ôćĺ                age = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - saved_datetime
   133Ôćĺ                age_str = f"{age.days}d {age.seconds // 3600}h"
   134Ôćĺ            else:
   135Ôćĺ                age_str = "unknown"
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ            return {
   138Ôćĺ                'saved_at': saved_at,
   139Ôćĺ                'age': age_str,
   140Ôćĺ                'cookie_count': len(cookies),
   141Ôćĺ            }
   142Ôćĺ        except Exception as e:
   143Ôćĺ            logger.error(f"Failed to get session info: {e}")
   144Ôćĺ            return None
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ# Global session manager instance
   148Ôćĺ_session_manager = None
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺdef get_session_manager() -> SessionManager:
   152Ôćĺ    """Get global session manager instance."""
   153Ôćĺ    global _session_manager
   154Ôćĺ    if _session_manager is None:
   155Ôćĺ        _session_manager = SessionManager()
   156Ôćĺ    return _session_manager

    10Ôćĺ

    14Ôćĺ@router.get("/")

1Ôćĺ"""Lightweight database migration system."""
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺfrom typing import Callable, List, Tuple
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺfrom sqlalchemy import text, inspect
     6Ôćĺfrom loguru import logger
     7Ôćĺ

     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺdef migration(version: int, description: str):
    12Ôćĺ    """Decorator to register a migration function."""
    13Ôćĺ    def decorator(fn: Callable):
    14Ôćĺ        _migrations.append((version, description, fn))
    15Ôćĺ        return fn
    16Ôćĺ    return decorator
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ@migration(1, "Initial schema ÔÇö create core tables if missing")
    20Ôćĺdef _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    21Ôćĺ    """Ensure core tables exist."""
    22Ôćĺ    pass
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ@migration(2, "Add retry_count column to messages table")
    26Ôćĺdef _m002_messages_retry_count(conn):
    27Ôćĺ    inspector = inspect(conn)
    28Ôćĺ    columns = [c["name"] for c in inspector.get_columns("messages")]
    29Ôćĺ    if "retry_count" not in columns:
    30Ôćĺ        conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0"))
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺdef _ensure_version_table(conn) -> None:
    34Ôćĺ    conn.execute(text("""
    35Ôćĺ        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
    36Ôćĺ            version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    37Ôćĺ            description TEXT NOT NULL,
    38Ôćĺ            applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    39Ôćĺ        )
    40Ôćĺ    """))
    41Ôćĺ    conn.commit()
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺdef _current_version(conn) -> int:
    45Ôćĺ    result = conn.execute(text("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"))
    46Ôćĺ    row = result.fetchone()
    47Ôćĺ    return row[0] or 0 if row else 0
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺdef run_migrations(engine) -> None:
    51Ôćĺ    """Apply all pending migrations."""
    52Ôćĺ    with engine.connect() as conn:
    53Ôćĺ        _ensure_version_table(conn)
    54Ôćĺ        current = _current_version(conn)
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ        pending = [(v, d, fn) for v, d, fn in _migrations if v > current]
    57Ôćĺ        if not pending:
    58Ôćĺ            logger.debug(f"Database schema is up-to-date (version {current})")
    59Ôćĺ            return
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ        for version, description, fn in sorted(pending):
    62Ôćĺ            logger.info(f"Applying migration v{version}: {description}")
    63Ôćĺ            fn(conn)
    64Ôćĺ            conn.execute(
    65Ôćĺ                text("INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description) VALUES (:v, :d)"),
    66Ôćĺ                {"v": version, "d": description},
    67Ôćĺ            )
    68Ôćĺ            conn.commit()
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ        final = _current_version(conn)
    71Ôćĺ        logger.info(f"Migrations complete ÔÇö schema version {final}")

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me compile the full dead code report.

---

## Dead Code Audit Report

### 1. Unused Imports

**`src/web/routes/auth.py` ÔÇö line 6**
`chrome_manager` is imported but never used in the file body. The `auth_status` and `auth_check` handlers call `get_browser_manager()` directly; `auth_logout` does not touch Chrome at all.
```python
from src.web.app import chrome_manager, templates   # chrome_manager unused
```

---

### 2. Unused Functions / Methods

**`src/utils/validators.py` ÔÇö `validate_boss_id` (line 9) and `validate_keywords` (line 34)**
Neither function is called from any `src/` file. The only callers are in `tests/test_validators.py`. No production code currently gates on these validators; e.g., the web routes use Pydantic schemas instead.

**`src/utils/delays.py` ÔÇö `get_random_delay` (line 60)**
Only called by `async_random_delay` internally (line 100). No external file imports or calls it directly. It is an internal helper but is exported at module level with no external callers ÔÇö borderline, but worth noting.

**`src/core/anti_detection.py` ÔÇö `human_scroll`, `human_click`, `human_type` (lines 21, 52, 74)**
The entire `anti_detection` module is never imported anywhere in `src/`. Neither production code nor tests reference it. All three public functions are dead from a live-code perspective.
```
grep result: "No matches found" for "from src.core.anti_detection"
```

**`src/utils/logger.py` ÔÇö `setup_logger` (line 11)**
Only referenced from `scripts/setup_db.py`. Not called anywhere in the `src/` tree, including `run_web.py` or `src/web/app.py`. Loguru is configured inline in each module via `logger.add()`. This function is stranded.

**`src/models/database.py` ÔÇö `DatabaseManager.drop_all` (lines 181-184)**
Never called from `src/`. Only used in test fixtures (`tests/conftest.py`, `tests/test_database.py`) via `Base.metadata.drop_all(engine)` ÔÇö not through this method. The method itself has no callers.

**`src/utils/rate_limiter.py` ÔÇö `reset_rate_limiter` (line 280)**
Defined but never called outside of its own file. No test or production code imports or calls it.

**`src/utils/config.py` ÔÇö `reset_config` (line 98)**
Defined but never called anywhere in `src/` or tests. Zero callers.

**`src/models/database.py` ÔÇö `reset_db_manager` (lines 199-202)**
Defined but never called anywhere in `src/` or tests. Zero callers.

**`src/utils/checkpoint.py` ÔÇö `CheckpointManager.total_results` property (lines 132-133)**
The `total_results` parameter is accepted by `CheckpointManager.save()` (line 44) but `pipeline.py` never passes it (it always uses the default `None`). The `total_results` property reader is consequently never accessed.

---

### 3. Commented-Out Code Blocks

No large blocks (3+ lines) of commented-out code were found in the reviewed files. All comment blocks are docstrings or inline explanations.

---

### 4. Unused Variables / Constants

**`src/utils/checkpoint.py` ÔÇö `mode` parameter and checkpoint field (lines 45, 61, 141)**
`mode` is accepted as a parameter to `CheckpointManager.save()`, written into the checkpoint JSON (line 61), and read back in `summary` (line 141). However, `pipeline.py` never passes `mode=` (always uses default `"recommend"`), and no caller ever reads `checkpoint.params.get("mode")` for any branching logic. It is stored and displayed but has no functional effect.

**`src/utils/checkpoint.py` ÔÇö `company` parameter (lines 35, 55)**
`company: tuple` is accepted by `save()` and serialized (`list(company) if company else []`). In `pipeline.py` the only call passes `company=()` (line 380). No part of resume logic reads `checkpoint.params["company"]`. It is a vestigial parameter from a prior design.

**`src/utils/checkpoint.py` ÔÇö `total_results` parameter (line 44)**
Same issue as the property above: always passed as `None` (default), serialized as `{}`, and the property is never read by live code.

---

### 5. Empty / Trivial Exception Blocks

**`src/models/migrations.py` ÔÇö `_m001_initial_schema` (lines 21-23)**
Migration v1 is registered with the decorator but the function body is literally `pass`. It performs no work, yet is iterated and committed as a migration version on every fresh install.
```python
@migration(1, "Initial schema ÔÇö create core tables if missing")
def _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    """Ensure core tables exist."""
    pass
```
This is not a safety issue, but it runs `conn.execute / conn.commit` for a no-op function.

**`src/modules/search.py` ÔÇö inner `except e: continue` (line 234)**
```python
except (e):
    continue
```
The `except` in the Strategy 2 fallback list extraction silently discards all errors with no logging. It is a true swallowed-exception anti-pattern.

---

### 6. Backwards-Compatibility Shims / Orphaned Artifacts

**`boss_flow_search.py` (root level)**
This is an auto-generated browser recording replay script. It duplicates a `goto` call twice (lines 16-17), uses hardcoded `asyncio.sleep` values (39.4s, etc.), and references selectors that depend on live page state. It is not imported or called by anything in the project; it is a debugging artifact with no production role.

**`src/models/migrations.py` ÔÇö `retry_count` column migration (lines 25-30)**
Migration v2 adds a `retry_count` column to the `messages` table. However, the `Message` ORM model in `database.py` has no `retry_count` column defined. The column is added to the SQLite table but never mapped or read ÔÇö the data goes nowhere.

**`src/utils/scoring.py` ÔÇö `_DEFAULT_WEIGHTS` (lines 78-87)**
This constant is used as the fallback when `settings.json` has no `scoring.weights` key. But `settings.json` already has a fully populated `scoring.weights` block, so `_DEFAULT_WEIGHTS` is never actually used in production. It is purely a safety net / documentation artifact.

---

### Summary Table

| # | File | Lines | Category | Severity |
|---|------|--------|----------|----------|
| 1 | `src/web/routes/auth.py` | 6 | Unused import (`chrome_manager`) | Low |
| 2 | `src/utils/validators.py` | 9, 34 | Unused functions (`validate_boss_id`, `validate_keywords` ÔÇö no prod callers) | Medium |
| 3 | `src/core/anti_detection.py` | 21, 52, 74 | Entire module unreferenced ÔÇö all 3 public functions dead | High |
| 4 | `src/utils/logger.py` | 11 | `setup_logger` ÔÇö not called from `src/` (only `scripts/`) | Low |
| 5 | `src/models/database.py` | 181ÔÇô184 | `DatabaseManager.drop_all` ÔÇö no callers | Low |
| 6 | `src/utils/rate_limiter.py` | 280ÔÇô283 | `reset_rate_limiter` ÔÇö no callers | Low |
| 7 | `src/utils/config.py` | 98ÔÇô101 | `reset_config` ÔÇö no callers | Low |
| 8 | `src/models/database.py` | 199ÔÇô202 | `reset_db_manager` ÔÇö no callers | Low |
| 9 | `src/utils/checkpoint.py` | 35, 44ÔÇô45, 55, 61, 68, 132ÔÇô133, 141 | `company`, `mode`, `total_results` params/properties ÔÇö accepted but never meaningfully used | Medium |
| 10 | `src/models/migrations.py` | 19ÔÇô23 | Migration v1 `pass` body ÔÇö no-op registered migration | Low |
| 11 | `src/modules/search.py` | 234 | Bare `except: continue` swallowing errors silently | Medium |
| 12 | `boss_flow_search.py` | entire file | Recording replay artifact ÔÇö not imported, duplicate gotos, hardcoded sleeps | Medium |
| 13 | `src/models/migrations.py` | 25ÔÇô30 | `retry_count` column added but ORM model has no such field ÔÇö orphaned migration | Medium |

### Priority Recommendations

1. **Delete `src/core/anti_detection.py`** ÔÇö 100% unreferenced. If Emunium-based interaction is needed later, it should be reintroduced intentionally.
2. **Delete or repurpose `boss_flow_search.py`** ÔÇö pure debug artifact in the repo root.
3. **Fix migration v2 / `retry_count` mismatch** ÔÇö either add `retry_count` to the `Message` ORM model or drop the migration. Currently the column exists in SQLite but is invisible to SQLAlchemy.
4. **Remove unused `company`, `mode`, `total_results` from `CheckpointManager.save()`** ÔÇö the API surface is misleading since pipeline.py never uses them.
5. **Remove `validate_boss_id` and `validate_keywords`** ÔÇö or wire them into the web/CLI layers where they would actually provide value.
6. **Remove the 5 reset/helper functions** (`reset_rate_limiter`, `reset_config`, `reset_db_manager`) and `DatabaseManager.drop_all` if they will not be used in tests ÔÇö currently they sit as dead surface area.
7. **Fix the silent `except: continue`** at `src/modules/search.py:234` ÔÇö at minimum add `logger.debug`.
